{{DEFAULTSORT:しんはる ぬやまこふんくん}} [[Category:Ancient Tombs in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:ill|'Kami Yadoru Shima' Munakata–Okinoshima Related Heritage Group|ja|「神宿る島」宗像・沖ノ島と関連遺産群}}]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites of Tombs]] [[Category:History of Fukutsu City]] [[Category:Kofun Groups]] [[category:goodbye $1]][[ja:新原・奴山古墳群]] [[Category:Ngoodbye $1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[sn:hinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ngoodbye $1]] [[Category:Old untranslated pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:hinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:Old untranslated pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:円墳]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:円墳]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:円墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:方墳]] [[sn:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]]

[[ja:%E6%96%B0%E5%8E%9F%E3%83%BB%E5%A5%B4%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[Category:goodbye $1]] [[Category:Shortest 250 pages as of 2025-02-19]] [[sn:hinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]] {{脚注の不足|date=2015年8月12日 (水) 16:00 (UTC)}} [[File:Shinbaru-Nuyama Necropolis 20161014.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Stone Monument|ja|木碑}} of the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group (新原・奴山古墳群)]] [[File:Shimbata-nuyama_kofun.jpg|alt=Distant View [ja] of the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group (新原・奴山古墳群)|thumb]]
'''Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group''' (新原・奴山古墳群) is an assemblage of ancient Kofuns ({{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) that forms one part of the {{ill|Tsuya-zaki Kofun Group|ja|津屋崎古墳群}}, located in the Tsushima–Miyan mountain system of {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}’s {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市}}. It has been designated a National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} (it is included within the Historic Site “{{ill|Tsuya-zaki Kofun Group|ja|津屋崎古墳群}}”). The tumuli were constructed from the latter half of the 5th century to the latter half of the 6th century during the middle-late phase of the {{ill|Kofun Period|ja|古墳時代}}, and those interred are presumed to have been members or affiliates of the {{ill|Munakata Clan|ja|宗像氏}}. The name of the kofun group derives from the fact that it spans the “Shinbara” area (the local name in Katsura) and the “Nuyama” district.
In {{ill|2017|ja|2017年}} ({{ill|Heisei 29|ja|平成29年}}), as one of the constituent assets of the {{ill|'Kami Yadoru Shima' Munakata–Okinoshima Related Heritage Group|ja|「神宿る島」宗像・沖ノ島と関連遺産群}}, the kofun group was inscribed as a {{ill|World Cultural Heritage|ja|世界文化遺産}}. This marks the first instance in Japan in which a group of kofun has been registered as a World Heritage Site.
== Overview == [http://www.okinoshima-heritage.jp/files/Pamphlet_14_file.pdf] occupies a hilly area stretching 800 meters east–west, on which 5 {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Tumuli|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, 1 {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}, and 41 {{ill|Round Tumuli|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} currently survive; these have been assigned sequential numbers for convenience. In addition, historical records, excavation surveys ({{ill|Excavation Survey|ja|発掘調査}}), topographical studies ({{ill|Topography|ja|地形}}) and surveying ({{ill|Surveying|ja|測量}}) have confirmed that another 18 tumuli—lost due to the reclamation of adjacent {{ill|paddy fields|ja|田圃}}—once existed, and these too were assigned sequential numbers.
At the time the tumuli were constructed, an inlet ({{ill|Cove|ja|入り江}}) adjoined the western side of the hill; this inlet, into which the present {{ill|Tsuyazaki Bay|ja|津屋崎湾}} penetrated deeply, gradually receded. During the {{ill|Edo Period|ja|江戸時代}}, the area was reclaimed ({{ill|Land Reclamation|ja|干拓}}) and converted into a {{ill|Salt Field|ja|塩田}}, and from the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治時代}} onward it has been transformed into {{ill|Paddy Fields|ja|水田}}.
Around the kofun group, several {{ill|farm ponds|ja|ため池}}—such as {{ill|Gekka-ike|ja|月花池}}, {{ill|Harada-ike|ja|原田池}}, {{ill|Manekike|ja|招池}}, and {{ill|Fushibara-ike|ja|伏原池}}—are scattered; these were constructed for irrigation purposes during the {{ill|Early Modern Period|ja|近世}} and are unrelated to the kofun. The hilly area is bisected by {{ill|National Route 495|ja|国道495号}}. The basalt ({{ill|Basalt|ja|玄武岩}}) used in the tumuli’s stone chambers is presumed to have been transported by boat from {{ill|Aishima (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|相島 (福岡県)}} in the {{ill|Genkai-nada|ja|玄界灘}}.
== List of Tumuli == * '''Tomb No. 1''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with an estimated total length of 50 meters; the square portion is lost. It was constructed in the first half of the 5th century and is covered with {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}}. * '''Tombs No. 2–6''' – These have not survived due to leveling for road construction and private facility development. * '''Tomb No. 7''' – A square tumulus with sides measuring 20–24 meters; it is the only square tumulus in the {{ill|Munakata Region|ja|宗像地方}}. Constructed in the first half of the 5th century, it yielded an iron ({{ill|Iron|ja|鉄}}) and an axe ({{ill|Axe|ja|斧}}) similar to those found in the ritual remains of {{ill|Okinoshima|ja|沖ノ島}}, suggesting that it may also have served as a ceremonial site. * '''Tombs No. 8–11''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 12''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 43 meters, constructed in the latter half of the 5th century. * '''Tombs No. 13–19''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 20''' – A medium-sized round tumulus covered with fukiishi, constructed in the 5th century. * '''Tomb No. 21''' – A round tumulus with a diameter of 17 meters, constructed in the first half of the 5th century. A stone monument ({{ill|Itaben|ja|板碑}}) from the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property, stands atop its mound. * '''Tomb No. 22''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with an estimated total length of 80 meters, although the square portion is lost. Constructed in the first half of the 5th century, it is the largest in the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group and ranks among the oldest. It was also surrounded by moats and embankments, and a stone shrine of the later-enshrined Nuno-den-gu was erected on its mound. * '''Tomb No. 23''' – A small round tumulus from the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 24''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 53 meters, constructed in the first half of the 5th century, and, along with Tomb No. 22, among the oldest. * '''Tomb No. 25''' – A medium-sized round tumulus covered with fukiishi, constructed in the 5th century. * '''Tombs No. 26–29''' – Small round tumuli built on the slopes of the hill, constructed in the latter half of the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 30''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 54 meters, constructed from the mid to late 5th century. * '''Tombs No. 31–43''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tombs Nos. 44–45''' – These have been lost due to leveling. * '''Tombs Nos. 46–48''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tombs Nos. 49–59''' – These have been lost due to the reclamation of paddy fields.
<gallery perrow="4" widths="175px" heights="175px"> File:新原・奴山古墳群20号墳.JPG|Tomb No. 20 File:新原・奴山古墳群21号墳.JPG|Tomb No. 21 File:新原・奴山古墳群22号墳.JPG|Tomb No. 22 File:新原・奴山古墳群34～36号墳.JPG|Tombs Nos. 34–36 </gallery>
During the selection process for designation, other tumuli—such as the {{ill|Sakurakyo Kofun|ja|桜京古墳}}, {{ill|Tōgō Takatsuka Kofun|ja|東郷高塚古墳}}, and those within the broader {{ill|Tsuya-zaki Kofun Group|ja|津屋崎古墳群}} (which encompasses the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group), including the Katsura Mine-no-Hata Kofun and {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳}}—were omitted from selection. The Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group was retained because its location, facing the sea ({{ill|Sea|ja|海}}) of the {{ill|Genkai-nada|ja|玄界灘}}, preserves a “landscape integrated with the sea” ({{ill|Cultural Landscape|ja|文化的景観}}) that embodies the maritime worship of the {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} (and its associated {{ill|Munakata Sanmeigen|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}), as explicitly stated in the recommendation documents. For these reasons, the more inland {{ill|Sakurakyo Kofun|ja|桜京古墳}} and {{ill|Tōgō Takatsuka Kofun|ja|東郷高塚古墳}} were excluded, and {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳}}—serving as the inner shrine of {{ill|Miyajidake Shrine|ja|宮地嶽神社}} and representing a different aspect of Munakata faith—was also left out.
In the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group, a designated viewscape preservation zone under the {{ill|Landscape Act|ja|景観法}} has been applied to conserve the agricultural lands, rural scenery, and overall landscape surrounding the tumuli—which overlooks {{ill|Oshima (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|大島 (福岡県)}}—and under the {{ill|Outdoor Advertising Law|ja|屋外広告物法}} and local ordinances, incongruous buildings and signboards are periodically removed.<ref>[http://www.city.fukutsu.lg.jp/pdf/kenkou/toshi/keikankeikaku01.pdf]{{ill|Fukutsu City Landscape Plan|ja|福津市景観計画}}</ref>
== Post-World Heritage Registration Developments == Following the heavy rains in {{ill|Heisei 30|ja|平成30年}} July, part of the tumuli collapsed.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20180711185327/https://this.kiji.is/389598562214937697?c=39546741839462401 Damage reported in the World Heritage kofun group, Fukuoka, {{ill|Kyodo News|ja|共同通信}}, July 11, 2018]</ref>
== Access == * From the Miyaji exit of {{ill|Fukuma Station|ja|福間駅}} on the {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}}, take the {{ill|Fukutsu Mini Bus, Katsura Line|ja|ふくつミニバス#勝浦線}} and alight at the Showa Gakuen-mae (Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group) stop. However, only three services operate per day from the station, and only three departures run from the Fukutsu City Complex Cultural Center (Camellia Stage)—the guidance facility for the kofun group—toward Katsura-hama (access to Camellia Stage is made via a connection from Fukuma Station on the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス}} heading to Tsuyazaki). Services do not operate on Sundays, public holidays, or during the New Year period. Because a one-box (minibus) vehicle is used, capacity is limited to 13 people, with priority given to elderly local residents who have voluntarily surrendered their driver's licenses and to those living in areas with insufficient public transport along the route. The fare is a flat rate of 200 yen. Note that the Showa Gakuen-mae (Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group) stop is also the nearest to the Nuyama Observatory, from which one can enjoy a distant view of the kofun group. * By car, it is approximately 13 km from the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}} {{ill|Koga Interchange|ja|古賀インターチェンジ}}.

Constructed in the first half of the 5th century, it yielded an iron ({{ill|Iron|ja|鉄}}) and an axe ({{ill|Axe|ja|斧}}) similar to those found in the ritual remains of {{ill|Okinoshima|ja|沖ノ島}}, suggesting that it may also have served as a ceremonial site. * '''Tombs No. 8–11''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 12''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 43 meters, constructed in the latter half of the 5th century. * '''Tombs No. 13–19''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 20''' – A medium-sized round tumulus covered with fukiishi, constructed in the 5th century. * '''Tomb No. 21''' – A round tumulus with a diameter of 17 meters, constructed in the first half of the 5th century. A stone monument ({{ill|Itaben|ja|板碑}}) from the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property, stands atop its mound. * '''Tomb No. 22''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with an estimated total length of 80 meters, although the square portion is lost. Constructed in the first half of the 5th century, it is the largest in the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group and ranks among the oldest. It was also surrounded by moats and embankments, and a stone shrine of the later-enshrined Nuno-den-gu was erected on its mound. * '''Tomb No. 23''' – A small round tumulus from the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 24''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 53 meters, constructed in the first half of the 5th century, and, along with Tomb No. 22, among the oldest. * '''Tomb No. 25''' – A medium-sized round tumulus covered with fukiishi, constructed in the 5th century. * '''Tombs No. 26–29''' – Small round tumuli built on the slopes of the hill, constructed in the latter half of the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 30''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 54 meters, constructed from the mid to late 5th century. * '''Tombs No. 31–43''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tombs Nos. 44–45''' – These have been lost due to leveling. * '''Tombs Nos. 46–48''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tombs Nos. 49–59''' – These have been lost due to the reclamation of paddy fields.
<gallery perrow="4" widths="175px" heights="175px"> File:新原・奴山古墳群20号墳.JPG|Tomb No. 20 File:新原・奴山古墳群21号墳.JPG|Tomb No. 21 File:新原・奴山古墳群22号墳.JPG|Tomb No. 22 File:新原・奴山古墳群34～36号墳.JPG|Tombs Nos. 34–36 </gallery>
During the selection process for designation, other tumuli—such as the {{ill|Sakurakyo Kofun|ja|桜京古墳}}, {{ill|Tōgō Takatsuka Kofun|ja|東郷高塚古墳}}, and those within the broader {{ill|Tsuya-zaki Kofun Group|ja|津屋崎古墳群}} (which encompasses the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group), including the Katsura Mine-no-Hata Kofun and {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳}}—were omitted from selection. The Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group was retained because its location, facing the sea ({{ill|Sea|ja|海}}) of the {{ill|Genkai-nada|ja|玄界灘}}, preserves a “landscape integrated with the sea” ({{ill|Cultural Landscape|ja|文化的景観}}) that embodies the maritime worship of the {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} (and its associated {{ill|Munakata Sanmeigen|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}), as explicitly stated in the recommendation documents. For these reasons, the more inland {{ill|Sakurakyo Kofun|ja|桜京古墳}} and {{ill|Tōgō Takatsuka Kofun|ja|東郷高塚古墳}} were excluded, and {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳}}—serving as the inner shrine of {{ill|Miyajidake Shrine|ja|宮地嶽神社}} and representing a different aspect of Munakata faith—was also left out.
In the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group, a designated viewscape preservation zone under the {{ill|Landscape Act|ja|景観法}} has been applied to conserve the agricultural lands, rural scenery, and overall landscape surrounding the tumuli—which overlooks {{ill|Oshima (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|大島 (福岡県)}}—and under the {{ill|Outdoor Advertising Law|ja|屋外広告物法}} and local ordinances, incongruous buildings and signboards are periodically removed.<ref>[http://www.city.fukutsu.lg.jp/pdf/kenkou/toshi/keikankeikaku01.pdf]{{ill|Fukutsu City Landscape Plan|ja|福津市景観計画}}</ref>
== Post-World Heritage Registration Developments == Following the heavy rains in {{ill|Heisei 30|ja|平成30年}} July, part of the tumuli collapsed.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20180711185327/https://this.kiji.is/389598562214937697?c=39546741839462401 Damage reported in the World Heritage kofun group, Fukuoka, {{ill|Kyodo News|ja|共同通信}}, July 11, 2018]</ref>
== Access == * From the Miyaji exit of {{ill|Fukuma Station|ja|福間駅}} on the {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}}, take the {{ill|Fukutsu Mini Bus, Katsura Line|ja|ふくつミニバス#勝浦線}} and alight at the Showa Gakuen-mae (Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group) stop. However, only three services operate per day from the station, and only three departures run from the Fukutsu City Complex Cultural Center (Camellia Stage)—the guidance facility for the kofun group—toward Katsura-hama (access to Camellia Stage is made via a connection from Fukuma Station on the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス}} heading to Tsuyazaki). Services do not operate on Sundays, public holidays, or during the New Year period. Because a one-box (minibus) vehicle is used, capacity is limited to 13 people, with priority given to elderly local residents who have voluntarily surrendered their driver's licenses and to those living in areas with insufficient public transport along the route. The fare is a flat rate of 200 yen. Note that the Showa Gakuen-mae (Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group) stop is also the nearest to the Nuyama Observatory, from which one can enjoy a distant view of the kofun group. * By car, it is approximately 13 km from the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}} {{ill|Koga Interchange|ja|古賀インターチェンジ}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Munakata Clan|ja|宗像氏}} * {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳}} * {{ill|List of Ancient Tombs in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧}}

revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:Translated pages]] to [[Category:completion tier 5]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[ja:%E6%96%B0%E5%8E%9F%E3%83%BB%E5%A5%B4%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[Category:goodbye $1]] [[Category:Shortest 250 pages as of 2025-02-19]] [[sn:hinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]] {{脚注の不足|date=2015年8月12日 (水) 16:00 (UTC)}} [[File:Shinbaru-Nuyama Necropolis 20161014.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Stone Monument|ja|木碑}} of the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group (新原・奴山古墳群)]] [[File:Shimbata-nuyama_kofun.jpg|alt=Distant View [ja] of the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group (新原・奴山古墳群)|thumb]]
'''Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group''' (新原・奴山古墳群) is an assemblage of ancient Kofuns ({{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) that forms one part of the {{ill|Tsuya-zaki Kofun Group|ja|津屋崎古墳群}}, located in the Tsushima–Miyan mountain system of {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}’s {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市}}. It has been designated a National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} (it is included within the Historic Site “{{ill|Tsuya-zaki Kofun Group|ja|津屋崎古墳群}}”). The tumuli were constructed from the latter half of the 5th century to the latter half of the 6th century during the middle-late phase of the {{ill|Kofun Period|ja|古墳時代}}, and those interred are presumed to have been members or affiliates of the {{ill|Munakata Clan|ja|宗像氏}}. The name of the kofun group derives from the fact that it spans the “Shinbara” area (the local name in Katsura) and the “Nuyama” district.
In {{ill|2017|ja|2017年}} ({{ill|Heisei 29|ja|平成29年}}), as one of the constituent assets of the {{ill|'Kami Yadoru Shima' Munakata–Okinoshima Related Heritage Group|ja|「神宿る島」宗像・沖ノ島と関連遺産群}}, the kofun group was inscribed as a {{ill|World Cultural Heritage|ja|世界文化遺産}}. This marks the first instance in Japan in which a group of kofun has been registered as a World Heritage Site.
== Overview == [http://www.okinoshima-heritage.jp/files/Pamphlet_14_file.pdf] occupies a hilly area stretching 800 meters east–west, on which 5 {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Tumuli|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, 1 {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}, and 41 {{ill|Round Tumuli|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} currently survive; these have been assigned sequential numbers for convenience. In addition, historical records, excavation surveys ({{ill|Excavation Survey|ja|発掘調査}}), topographical studies ({{ill|Topography|ja|地形}}) and surveying ({{ill|Surveying|ja|測量}}) have confirmed that another 18 tumuli—lost due to the reclamation of adjacent {{ill|paddy fields|ja|田圃}}—once existed, and these too were assigned sequential numbers.
At the time the tumuli were constructed, an inlet ({{ill|Cove|ja|入り江}}) adjoined the western side of the hill; this inlet, into which the present {{ill|Tsuyazaki Bay|ja|津屋崎湾}} penetrated deeply, gradually receded. During the {{ill|Edo Period|ja|江戸時代}}, the area was reclaimed ({{ill|Land Reclamation|ja|干拓}}) and converted into a {{ill|Salt Field|ja|塩田}}, and from the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治時代}} onward it has been transformed into {{ill|Paddy Fields|ja|水田}}.
Around the kofun group, several {{ill|farm ponds|ja|ため池}}—such as {{ill|Gekka-ike|ja|月花池}}, {{ill|Harada-ike|ja|原田池}}, {{ill|Manekike|ja|招池}}, and {{ill|Fushibara-ike|ja|伏原池}}—are scattered; these were constructed for irrigation purposes during the {{ill|Early Modern Period|ja|近世}} and are unrelated to the kofun. The hilly area is bisected by {{ill|National Route 495|ja|国道495号}}. The basalt ({{ill|Basalt|ja|玄武岩}}) used in the tumuli’s stone chambers is presumed to have been transported by boat from {{ill|Aishima (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|相島 (福岡県)}} in the {{ill|Genkai-nada|ja|玄界灘}}.
== List of Tumuli == * '''Tomb No. 1''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with an estimated total length of 50 meters; the square portion is lost. It was constructed in the first half of the 5th century and is covered with {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}}. * '''Tombs No. 2–6''' – These have not survived due to leveling for road construction and private facility development. * '''Tomb No. 7''' – A square tumulus with sides measuring 20–24 meters; it is the only square tumulus in the {{ill|Munakata Region|ja|宗像地方}}. Constructed in the first half of the 5th century, it yielded an iron ({{ill|Iron|ja|鉄}}) and an axe ({{ill|Axe|ja|斧}}) similar to those found in the ritual remains of {{ill|Okinoshima|ja|沖ノ島}}, suggesting that it may also have served as a ceremonial site. * '''Tombs No. 8–11''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 12''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 43 meters, constructed in the latter half of the 5th century. * '''Tombs No. 13–19''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 20''' – A medium-sized round tumulus covered with fukiishi, constructed in the 5th century. * '''Tomb No. 21''' – A round tumulus with a diameter of 17 meters, constructed in the first half of the 5th century. A stone monument ({{ill|Itaben|ja|板碑}}) from the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property, stands atop its mound. * '''Tomb No. 22''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with an estimated total length of 80 meters, although the square portion is lost. Constructed in the first half of the 5th century, it is the largest in the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group and ranks among the oldest. It was also surrounded by moats and embankments, and a stone shrine of the later-enshrined Nuno-den-gu was erected on its mound. * '''Tomb No. 23''' – A small round tumulus from the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 24''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 53 meters, constructed in the first half of the 5th century, and, along with Tomb No. 22, among the oldest. * '''Tomb No. 25''' – A medium-sized round tumulus covered with fukiishi, constructed in the 5th century. * '''Tombs No. 26–29''' – Small round tumuli built on the slopes of the hill, constructed in the latter half of the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 30''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 54 meters, constructed from the mid to late 5th century. * '''Tombs No. 31–43''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tombs Nos. 44–45''' – These have been lost due to leveling. * '''Tombs Nos. 46–48''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tombs Nos. 49–59''' – These have been lost due to the reclamation of paddy fields.
<gallery perrow="4" widths="175px" heights="175px"> File:新原・奴山古墳群20号墳.JPG|Tomb No. 20 File:新原・奴山古墳群21号墳.JPG|Tomb No. 21 File:新原・奴山古墳群22号墳.JPG|Tomb No. 22 File:新原・奴山古墳群34～36号墳.JPG|Tombs Nos. 34–36 </gallery>
During the selection process for designation, other tumuli—such as the {{ill|Sakurakyo Kofun|ja|桜京古墳}}, {{ill|Tōgō Takatsuka Kofun|ja|東郷高塚古墳}}, and those within the broader {{ill|Tsuya-zaki Kofun Group|ja|津屋崎古墳群}} (which encompasses the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group), including the Katsura Mine-no-Hata Kofun and {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳}}—were omitted from selection. The Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group was retained because its location, facing the sea ({{ill|Sea|ja|海}}) of the {{ill|Genkai-nada|ja|玄界灘}}, preserves a “landscape integrated with the sea” ({{ill|Cultural Landscape|ja|文化的景観}}) that embodies the maritime worship of the {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} (and its associated {{ill|Munakata Sanmeigen|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}), as explicitly stated in the recommendation documents. For these reasons, the more inland {{ill|Sakurakyo Kofun|ja|桜京古墳}} and {{ill|Tōgō Takatsuka Kofun|ja|東郷高塚古墳}} were excluded, and {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳}}—serving as the inner shrine of {{ill|Miyajidake Shrine|ja|宮地嶽神社}} and representing a different aspect of Munakata faith—was also left out.
In the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group, a designated viewscape preservation zone under the {{ill|Landscape Act|ja|景観法}} has been applied to conserve the agricultural lands, rural scenery, and overall landscape surrounding the tumuli—which overlooks {{ill|Oshima (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|大島 (福岡県)}}—and under the {{ill|Outdoor Advertising Law|ja|屋外広告物法}} and local ordinances, incongruous buildings and signboards are periodically removed.<ref>[http://www.city.fukutsu.lg.jp/pdf/kenkou/toshi/keikankeikaku01.pdf]{{ill|Fukutsu City Landscape Plan|ja|福津市景観計画}}</ref>
== Post-World Heritage Registration Developments == Following the heavy rains in {{ill|Heisei 30|ja|平成30年}} July, part of the tumuli collapsed.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20180711185327/https://this.kiji.is/389598562214937697?c=39546741839462401 Damage reported in the World Heritage kofun group, Fukuoka, {{ill|Kyodo News|ja|共同通信}}, July 11, 2018]</ref>
== Access == * From the Miyaji exit of {{ill|Fukuma Station|ja|福間駅}} on the {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}}, take the {{ill|Fukutsu Mini Bus, Katsura Line|ja|ふくつミニバス#勝浦線}} and alight at the Showa Gakuen-mae (Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group) stop. However, only three services operate per day from the station, and only three departures run from the Fukutsu City Complex Cultural Center (Camellia Stage)—the guidance facility for the kofun group—toward Katsura-hama (access to Camellia Stage is made via a connection from Fukuma Station on the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス}} heading to Tsuyazaki). Services do not operate on Sundays, public holidays, or during the New Year period. Because a one-box (minibus) vehicle is used, capacity is limited to 13 people, with priority given to elderly local residents who have voluntarily surrendered their driver's licenses and to those living in areas with insufficient public transport along the route. The fare is a flat rate of 200 yen. Note that the Showa Gakuen-mae (Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group) stop is also the nearest to the Nuyama Observatory, from which one can enjoy a distant view of the kofun group. * By car, it is approximately 13 km from the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}} {{ill|Koga Interchange|ja|古賀インターチェンジ}}.

[[ja:%E6%96%B0%E5%8E%9F%E3%83%BB%E5%A5%B4%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[Category:Shortest 250 pages as of 2025-02-19]] [[sn:hinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]] {{脚注の不足|date=2015年8月12日 (水) 16:00 (UTC)}} [[File:Shinbaru-Nuyama Necropolis 20161014.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Stone Monument|ja|木碑}} of the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group (新原・奴山古墳群)]] [[File:Shimbata-nuyama_kofun.jpg|alt=Distant View [ja] of the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group (新原・奴山古墳群)|thumb]]
'''Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group''' (新原・奴山古墳群) is an assemblage of ancient Kofuns ({{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) that forms one part of the {{ill|Tsuya-zaki Kofun Group|ja|津屋崎古墳群}}, located in the Tsushima–Miyan mountain system of {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}’s {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市}}. It has been designated a National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} (it is included within the Historic Site “{{ill|Tsuya-zaki Kofun Group|ja|津屋崎古墳群}}”). The tumuli were constructed from the latter half of the 5th century to the latter half of the 6th century during the middle-late phase of the {{ill|Kofun Period|ja|古墳時代}}, and those interred are presumed to have been members or affiliates of the {{ill|Munakata Clan|ja|宗像氏}}. The name of the kofun group derives from the fact that it spans the “Shinbara” area (the local name in Katsura) and the “Nuyama” district.
In {{ill|2017|ja|2017年}} ({{ill|Heisei 29|ja|平成29年}}), as one of the constituent assets of the {{ill|'Kami Yadoru Shima' Munakata–Okinoshima Related Heritage Group|ja|「神宿る島」宗像・沖ノ島と関連遺産群}}, the kofun group was inscribed as a {{ill|World Cultural Heritage|ja|世界文化遺産}}. This marks the first instance in Japan in which a group of kofun has been registered as a World Heritage Site.
== Overview == [http://www.okinoshima-heritage.jp/files/Pamphlet_14_file.pdf] occupies a hilly area stretching 800 meters east–west, on which 5 {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Tumuli|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, 1 {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}, and 41 {{ill|Round Tumuli|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} currently survive; these have been assigned sequential numbers for convenience. In addition, historical records, excavation surveys ({{ill|Excavation Survey|ja|発掘調査}}), topographical studies ({{ill|Topography|ja|地形}}) and surveying ({{ill|Surveying|ja|測量}}) have confirmed that another 18 tumuli—lost due to the reclamation of adjacent {{ill|paddy fields|ja|田圃}}—once existed, and these too were assigned sequential numbers.
At the time the tumuli were constructed, an inlet ({{ill|Cove|ja|入り江}}) adjoined the western side of the hill; this inlet, into which the present {{ill|Tsuyazaki Bay|ja|津屋崎湾}} penetrated deeply, gradually receded. During the {{ill|Edo Period|ja|江戸時代}}, the area was reclaimed ({{ill|Land Reclamation|ja|干拓}}) and converted into a {{ill|Salt Field|ja|塩田}}, and from the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治時代}} onward it has been transformed into {{ill|Paddy Fields|ja|水田}}.
Around the kofun group, several {{ill|farm ponds|ja|ため池}}—such as {{ill|Gekka-ike|ja|月花池}}, {{ill|Harada-ike|ja|原田池}}, {{ill|Manekike|ja|招池}}, and {{ill|Fushibara-ike|ja|伏原池}}—are scattered; these were constructed for irrigation purposes during the {{ill|Early Modern Period|ja|近世}} and are unrelated to the kofun. The hilly area is bisected by {{ill|National Route 495|ja|国道495号}}. The basalt ({{ill|Basalt|ja|玄武岩}}) used in the tumuli’s stone chambers is presumed to have been transported by boat from {{ill|Aishima (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|相島 (福岡県)}} in the {{ill|Genkai-nada|ja|玄界灘}}.
== List of Tumuli == * '''Tomb No. 1''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with an estimated total length of 50 meters; the square portion is lost. It was constructed in the first half of the 5th century and is covered with {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}}. * '''Tombs No. 2–6''' – These have not survived due to leveling for road construction and private facility development. * '''Tomb No. 7''' – A square tumulus with sides measuring 20–24 meters; it is the only square tumulus in the {{ill|Munakata Region|ja|宗像地方}}. Constructed in the first half of the 5th century, it yielded an iron ({{ill|Iron|ja|鉄}}) and an axe ({{ill|Axe|ja|斧}}) similar to those found in the ritual remains of {{ill|Okinoshima|ja|沖ノ島}}, suggesting that it may also have served as a ceremonial site. * '''Tombs No. 8–11''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 12''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 43 meters, constructed in the latter half of the 5th century. * '''Tombs No. 13–19''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 20''' – A medium-sized round tumulus covered with fukiishi, constructed in the 5th century. * '''Tomb No. 21''' – A round tumulus with a diameter of 17 meters, constructed in the first half of the 5th century. A stone monument ({{ill|Itaben|ja|板碑}}) from the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property, stands atop its mound. * '''Tomb No. 22''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with an estimated total length of 80 meters, although the square portion is lost. Constructed in the first half of the 5th century, it is the largest in the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group and ranks among the oldest. It was also surrounded by moats and embankments, and a stone shrine of the later-enshrined Nuno-den-gu was erected on its mound. * '''Tomb No. 23''' – A small round tumulus from the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 24''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 53 meters, constructed in the first half of the 5th century, and, along with Tomb No. 22, among the oldest. * '''Tomb No. 25''' – A medium-sized round tumulus covered with fukiishi, constructed in the 5th century. * '''Tombs No. 26–29''' – Small round tumuli built on the slopes of the hill, constructed in the latter half of the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 30''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 54 meters, constructed from the mid to late 5th century. * '''Tombs No. 31–43''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tombs Nos. 44–45''' – These have been lost due to leveling. * '''Tombs Nos. 46–48''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tombs Nos. 49–59''' – These have been lost due to the reclamation of paddy fields.
<gallery perrow="4" widths="175px" heights="175px"> File:新原・奴山古墳群20号墳.JPG|Tomb No. 20 File:新原・奴山古墳群21号墳.JPG|Tomb No. 21 File:新原・奴山古墳群22号墳.JPG|Tomb No. 22 File:新原・奴山古墳群34～36号墳.JPG|Tombs Nos. 34–36 </gallery>
During the selection process for designation, other tumuli—such as the {{ill|Sakurakyo Kofun|ja|桜京古墳}}, {{ill|Tōgō Takatsuka Kofun|ja|東郷高塚古墳}}, and those within the broader {{ill|Tsuya-zaki Kofun Group|ja|津屋崎古墳群}} (which encompasses the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group), including the Katsura Mine-no-Hata Kofun and {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳}}—were omitted from selection. The Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group was retained because its location, facing the sea ({{ill|Sea|ja|海}}) of the {{ill|Genkai-nada|ja|玄界灘}}, preserves a “landscape integrated with the sea” ({{ill|Cultural Landscape|ja|文化的景観}}) that embodies the maritime worship of the {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} (and its associated {{ill|Munakata Sanmeigen|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}), as explicitly stated in the recommendation documents. For these reasons, the more inland {{ill|Sakurakyo Kofun|ja|桜京古墳}} and {{ill|Tōgō Takatsuka Kofun|ja|東郷高塚古墳}} were excluded, and {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳}}—serving as the inner shrine of {{ill|Miyajidake Shrine|ja|宮地嶽神社}} and representing a different aspect of Munakata faith—was also left out.
In the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group, a designated viewscape preservation zone under the {{ill|Landscape Act|ja|景観法}} has been applied to conserve the agricultural lands, rural scenery, and overall landscape surrounding the tumuli—which overlooks {{ill|Oshima (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|大島 (福岡県)}}—and under the {{ill|Outdoor Advertising Law|ja|屋外広告物法}} and local ordinances, incongruous buildings and signboards are periodically removed.<ref>[http://www.city.fukutsu.lg.jp/pdf/kenkou/toshi/keikankeikaku01.pdf]{{ill|Fukutsu City Landscape Plan|ja|福津市景観計画}}</ref>
== Post-World Heritage Registration Developments == Following the heavy rains in {{ill|Heisei 30|ja|平成30年}} July, part of the tumuli collapsed.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20180711185327/https://this.kiji.is/389598562214937697?c=39546741839462401 Damage reported in the World Heritage kofun group, Fukuoka, {{ill|Kyodo News|ja|共同通信}}, July 11, 2018]</ref>
== Access == * From the Miyaji exit of {{ill|Fukuma Station|ja|福間駅}} on the {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}}, take the {{ill|Fukutsu Mini Bus, Katsura Line|ja|ふくつミニバス#勝浦線}} and alight at the Showa Gakuen-mae (Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group) stop. However, only three services operate per day from the station, and only three departures run from the Fukutsu City Complex Cultural Center (Camellia Stage)—the guidance facility for the kofun group—toward Katsura-hama (access to Camellia Stage is made via a connection from Fukuma Station on the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス}} heading to Tsuyazaki). Services do not operate on Sundays, public holidays, or during the New Year period. Because a one-box (minibus) vehicle is used, capacity is limited to 13 people, with priority given to elderly local residents who have voluntarily surrendered their driver's licenses and to those living in areas with insufficient public transport along the route. The fare is a flat rate of 200 yen. Note that the Showa Gakuen-mae (Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group) stop is also the nearest to the Nuyama Observatory, from which one can enjoy a distant view of the kofun group. * By car, it is approximately 13 km from the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}} {{ill|Koga Interchange|ja|古賀インターチェンジ}}.

'''Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group''' (新原・奴山古墳群) is an assemblage of ancient Kofuns ({{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) that forms one part of the {{ill|Tsuya-zaki Kofun Group|ja|津屋崎古墳群}}, located in the Tsushima–Miyan mountain system of {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}’s {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市}}. It has been designated a National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} (it is included within the Historic Site “{{ill|Tsuya-zaki Kofun Group|ja|津屋崎古墳群}}”). The tumuli were constructed from the latter half of the 5th century to the latter half of the 6th century during the middle-late phase of the {{ill|Kofun Period|ja|古墳時代}}, and those interred are presumed to have been members or affiliates of the {{ill|Munakata Clan|ja|宗像氏}}. The name of the kofun group derives from the fact that it spans the “Shinbara” area (the local name in Katsura) and the “Nuyama” district.
In {{ill|2017|ja|2017年}} ({{ill|Heisei 29|ja|平成29年}}), as one of the constituent assets of the {{ill|'Kami Yadoru Shima' Munakata–Okinoshima Related Heritage Group|ja|「神宿る島」宗像・沖ノ島と関連遺産群}}, the kofun group was inscribed as a {{ill|World Cultural Heritage|ja|世界文化遺産}}. This marks the first instance in Japan in which a group of kofun has been registered as a World Heritage Site.

In addition, historical records, excavation surveys ({{ill|Excavation Survey|ja|発掘調査}}), topographical studies ({{ill|Topography|ja|地形}}) and surveying ({{ill|Surveying|ja|測量}}) have confirmed that another 18 tumuli—lost due to the reclamation of adjacent {{ill|paddy fields|ja|田圃}}—once existed, and these too were assigned sequential numbers.
At the time the tumuli were constructed, an inlet ({{ill|Cove|ja|入り江}}) adjoined the western side of the hill; this inlet, into which the present {{ill|Tsuyazaki Bay|ja|津屋崎湾}} penetrated deeply, gradually receded. Around the kofun group, several {{ill|farm ponds|ja|ため池}}—such as {{ill|Gekka-ike|ja|月花池}}, {{ill|Harada-ike|ja|原田池}}, {{ill|Manekike|ja|招池}}, and {{ill|Fushibara-ike|ja|伏原池}}—are scattered; these were constructed for irrigation purposes during the {{ill|Early Modern Period|ja|近世}} and are unrelated to the kofun. [[ja:%E6%96%B0%E5%8E%9F%E3%83%BB%E5%A5%B4%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[Category:Shortest 250 pages as of 2025-02-19]] [[sn:hinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]] {{脚注の不足|date=2015年8月12日 (水) 16:00 (UTC)}} [[File:Shinbaru-Nuyama Necropolis 20161014.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Stone Monument|ja|木碑}} of the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group (新原・奴山古墳群)]] [[File:Shimbata-nuyama_kofun.jpg|alt=Distant View [ja] of the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group (新原・奴山古墳群)|thumb]]
'''Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group''' (新原・奴山古墳群) is an assemblage of ancient Kofuns ({{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) that forms one part of the {{ill|Tsuya-zaki Kofun Group|ja|津屋崎古墳群}}, located in the Tsushima–Miyan mountain system of {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}’s {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市}}. It has been designated a National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} (it is included within the Historic Site “{{ill|Tsuya-zaki Kofun Group|ja|津屋崎古墳群}}”). The tumuli were constructed from the latter half of the 5th century to the latter half of the 6th century during the middle-late phase of the {{ill|Kofun Period|ja|古墳時代}}, and those interred are presumed to have been members or affiliates of the {{ill|Munakata Clan|ja|宗像氏}}. The name of the kofun group derives from the fact that it spans the “Shinbara” area (the local name in Katsura) and the “Nuyama” district.
In {{ill|2017|ja|2017年}} ({{ill|Heisei 29|ja|平成29年}}), as one of the constituent assets of the {{ill|'Kami Yadoru Shima' Munakata–Okinoshima Related Heritage Group|ja|「神宿る島」宗像・沖ノ島と関連遺産群}}, the kofun group was inscribed as a {{ill|World Cultural Heritage|ja|世界文化遺産}}. This marks the first instance in Japan in which a group of kofun has been registered as a World Heritage Site.
== Overview == [http://www.okinoshima-heritage.jp/files/Pamphlet_14_file.pdf] occupies a hilly area stretching 800 meters east–west, on which 5 {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Tumuli|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, 1 {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}, and 41 {{ill|Round Tumuli|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} currently survive; these have been assigned sequential numbers for convenience. In addition, historical records, excavation surveys ({{ill|Excavation Survey|ja|発掘調査}}), topographical studies ({{ill|Topography|ja|地形}}) and surveying ({{ill|Surveying|ja|測量}}) have confirmed that another 18 tumuli—lost due to the reclamation of adjacent {{ill|paddy fields|ja|田圃}}—once existed, and these too were assigned sequential numbers.
At the time the tumuli were constructed, an inlet ({{ill|Cove|ja|入り江}}) adjoined the western side of the hill; this inlet, into which the present {{ill|Tsuyazaki Bay|ja|津屋崎湾}} penetrated deeply, gradually receded. During the {{ill|Edo Period|ja|江戸時代}}, the area was reclaimed ({{ill|Land Reclamation|ja|干拓}}) and converted into a {{ill|Salt Field|ja|塩田}}, and from the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治時代}} onward it has been transformed into {{ill|Paddy Fields|ja|水田}}.
Around the kofun group, several {{ill|farm ponds|ja|ため池}}—such as {{ill|Gekka-ike|ja|月花池}}, {{ill|Harada-ike|ja|原田池}}, {{ill|Manekike|ja|招池}}, and {{ill|Fushibara-ike|ja|伏原池}}—are scattered; these were constructed for irrigation purposes during the {{ill|Early Modern Period|ja|近世}} and are unrelated to the kofun. The hilly area is bisected by {{ill|National Route 495|ja|国道495号}}. The basalt ({{ill|Basalt|ja|玄武岩}}) used in the tumuli’s stone chambers is presumed to have been transported by boat from {{ill|Aishima (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|相島 (福岡県)}} in the {{ill|Genkai-nada|ja|玄界灘}}.
== List of Tumuli == * '''Tomb No. 1''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with an estimated total length of 50 meters; the square portion is lost. It was constructed in the first half of the 5th century and is covered with {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}}. * '''Tombs No. 2–6''' – These have not survived due to leveling for road construction and private facility development. * '''Tomb No. 7''' – A square tumulus with sides measuring 20–24 meters; it is the only square tumulus in the {{ill|Munakata Region|ja|宗像地方}}. Constructed in the first half of the 5th century, it yielded an iron ({{ill|Iron|ja|鉄}}) and an axe ({{ill|Axe|ja|斧}}) similar to those found in the ritual remains of {{ill|Okinoshima|ja|沖ノ島}}, suggesting that it may also have served as a ceremonial site. * '''Tombs No. 8–11''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 12''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 43 meters, constructed in the latter half of the 5th century. * '''Tombs No. 13–19''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 20''' – A medium-sized round tumulus covered with fukiishi, constructed in the 5th century. * '''Tomb No. 21''' – A round tumulus with a diameter of 17 meters, constructed in the first half of the 5th century. A stone monument ({{ill|Itaben|ja|板碑}}) from the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property, stands atop its mound. * '''Tomb No. 22''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with an estimated total length of 80 meters, although the square portion is lost. Constructed in the first half of the 5th century, it is the largest in the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group and ranks among the oldest. It was also surrounded by moats and embankments, and a stone shrine of the later-enshrined Nuno-den-gu was erected on its mound. * '''Tomb No. 23''' – A small round tumulus from the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 24''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 53 meters, constructed in the first half of the 5th century, and, along with Tomb No. 22, among the oldest. * '''Tomb No. 25''' – A medium-sized round tumulus covered with fukiishi, constructed in the 5th century. * '''Tombs No. 26–29''' – Small round tumuli built on the slopes of the hill, constructed in the latter half of the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 30''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 54 meters, constructed from the mid to late 5th century. * '''Tombs No. 31–43''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tombs Nos. 44–45''' – These have been lost due to leveling. * '''Tombs Nos. 46–48''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tombs Nos. 49–59''' – These have been lost due to the reclamation of paddy fields.
<gallery perrow="4" widths="175px" heights="175px"> File:新原・奴山古墳群20号墳.JPG|Tomb No. 20 File:新原・奴山古墳群21号墳.JPG|Tomb No. 21 File:新原・奴山古墳群22号墳.JPG|Tomb No. 22 File:新原・奴山古墳群34～36号墳.JPG|Tombs Nos. 34–36 </gallery>
During the selection process for designation, other tumuli—such as the {{ill|Sakurakyo Kofun|ja|桜京古墳}}, {{ill|Tōgō Takatsuka Kofun|ja|東郷高塚古墳}}, and those within the broader {{ill|Tsuya-zaki Kofun Group|ja|津屋崎古墳群}} (which encompasses the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group), including the Katsura Mine-no-Hata Kofun and {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳}}—were omitted from selection. The Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group was retained because its location, facing the sea ({{ill|Sea|ja|海}}) of the {{ill|Genkai-nada|ja|玄界灘}}, preserves a “landscape integrated with the sea” ({{ill|Cultural Landscape|ja|文化的景観}}) that embodies the maritime worship of the {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} (and its associated {{ill|Munakata Sanmeigen|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}), as explicitly stated in the recommendation documents. For these reasons, the more inland {{ill|Sakurakyo Kofun|ja|桜京古墳}} and {{ill|Tōgō Takatsuka Kofun|ja|東郷高塚古墳}} were excluded, and {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳}}—serving as the inner shrine of {{ill|Miyajidake Shrine|ja|宮地嶽神社}} and representing a different aspect of Munakata faith—was also left out.
In the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group, a designated viewscape preservation zone under the {{ill|Landscape Act|ja|景観法}} has been applied to conserve the agricultural lands, rural scenery, and overall landscape surrounding the tumuli—which overlooks {{ill|Oshima (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|大島 (福岡県)}}—and under the {{ill|Outdoor Advertising Law|ja|屋外広告物法}} and local ordinances, incongruous buildings and signboards are periodically removed.<ref>[http://www.city.fukutsu.lg.jp/pdf/kenkou/toshi/keikankeikaku01.pdf]{{ill|Fukutsu City Landscape Plan|ja|福津市景観計画}}</ref>
== Post-World Heritage Registration Developments == Following the heavy rains in {{ill|Heisei 30|ja|平成30年}} July, part of the tumuli collapsed.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20180711185327/https://this.kiji.is/389598562214937697?c=39546741839462401 Damage reported in the World Heritage kofun group, Fukuoka, {{ill|Kyodo News|ja|共同通信}}, July 11, 2018]</ref>
== Access == * From the Miyaji exit of {{ill|Fukuma Station|ja|福間駅}} on the {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}}, take the {{ill|Fukutsu Mini Bus, Katsura Line|ja|ふくつミニバス#勝浦線}} and alight at the Showa Gakuen-mae (Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group) stop. However, only three services operate per day from the station, and only three departures run from the Fukutsu City Complex Cultural Center (Camellia Stage)—the guidance facility for the kofun group—toward Katsura-hama (access to Camellia Stage is made via a connection from Fukuma Station on the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス}} heading to Tsuyazaki). Services do not operate on Sundays, public holidays, or during the New Year period. Because a one-box (minibus) vehicle is used, capacity is limited to 13 people, with priority given to elderly local residents who have voluntarily surrendered their driver's licenses and to those living in areas with insufficient public transport along the route. The fare is a flat rate of 200 yen. Note that the Showa Gakuen-mae (Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group) stop is also the nearest to the Nuyama Observatory, from which one can enjoy a distant view of the kofun group. * By car, it is approximately 13 km from the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}} {{ill|Koga Interchange|ja|古賀インターチェンジ}}.

The Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group was retained because its location, facing the sea ({{ill|Sea|ja|海}}) of the {{ill|Genkai-nada|ja|玄界灘}}, preserves a “landscape integrated with the sea” ({{ill|Cultural Landscape|ja|文化的景観}}) that embodies the maritime worship of the {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} (and its associated {{ill|Munakata Sanmeigen|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}), as explicitly stated in the recommendation documents. For these reasons, the more inland {{ill|Sakurakyo Kofun|ja|桜京古墳}} and {{ill|Tōgō Takatsuka Kofun|ja|東郷高塚古墳}} were excluded, and {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳}}—serving as the inner shrine of {{ill|Miyajidake Shrine|ja|宮地嶽神社}} and representing a different aspect of Munakata faith—was also left out.
In the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group, a designated viewscape preservation zone under the {{ill|Landscape Act|ja|景観法}} has been applied to conserve the agricultural lands, rural scenery, and overall landscape surrounding the tumuli—which overlooks {{ill|Oshima (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|大島 (福岡県)}}—and under the {{ill|Outdoor Advertising Law|ja|屋外広告物法}} and local ordinances, incongruous buildings and signboards are periodically removed.<ref>[http://www.city.fukutsu.lg.jp/pdf/kenkou/toshi/keikankeikaku01.pdf]{{ill|Fukutsu City Landscape Plan|ja|福津市景観計画}}</ref>
== Post-World Heritage Registration Developments == Following the heavy rains in {{ill|Heisei 30|ja|平成30年}} July, part of the tumuli collapsed.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20180711185327/https://this.kiji.is/389598562214937697?c=39546741839462401 Damage reported in the World Heritage kofun group, Fukuoka, {{ill|Kyodo News|ja|共同通信}}, July 11, 2018]</ref>
== Access == * From the Miyaji exit of {{ill|Fukuma Station|ja|福間駅}} on the {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}}, take the {{ill|Fukutsu Mini Bus, Katsura Line|ja|ふくつミニバス#勝浦線}} and alight at the Showa Gakuen-mae (Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group) stop. However, only three services operate per day from the station, and only three departures run from the Fukutsu City Complex Cultural Center (Camellia Stage)—the guidance facility for the kofun group—toward Katsura-hama (access to Camellia Stage is made via a connection from Fukuma Station on the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス}} heading to Tsuyazaki). Services do not operate on Sundays, public holidays, or during the New Year period. Because a one-box (minibus) vehicle is used, capacity is limited to 13 people, with priority given to elderly local residents who have voluntarily surrendered their driver's licenses and to those living in areas with insufficient public transport along the route. The fare is a flat rate of 200 yen. Note that the Showa Gakuen-mae (Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group) stop is also the nearest to the Nuyama Observatory, from which one can enjoy a distant view of the kofun group. * By car, it is approximately 13 km from the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}} {{ill|Koga Interchange|ja|古賀インターチェンジ}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Munakata Clan|ja|宗像氏}} * {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳}} * {{ill|List of Ancient Tombs in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧}}
== External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/207243] ({{ill|Cultural Heritage Online|ja|文化遺産オンライン}}) * [http://www.fsg.pref.fukuoka.jp/bunka/index.asp]
{{coord|33|49|03|N|130|29|09|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=title|name=新原・奴山古墳群}}
{{japanese-history-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:しんはる ぬやまこふんくん}} [[Category:Ancient Tombs in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:ill|'Kami Yadoru Shima' Munakata–Okinoshima Related Heritage Group|ja|「神宿る島」宗像・沖ノ島と関連遺産群}}]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites of Tombs]] [[Category:History of Fukutsu City]] [[Category:Kofun Groups]] [[ja:新原・奴山古墳群]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[sn:hinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Old untranslated pages]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]] [[sn:hinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:Old untranslated pages]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:方墳]] [[sn:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[sn:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[sn:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:方墳]][[sn:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:方墳]] [[sn:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]][[sn:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]] [[Category:c]][[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:C tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[en:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]]

[[ja:%E6%96%B0%E5%8E%9F%E3%83%BB%E5%A5%B4%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[Category:Shortest 250 pages as of 2025-02-19]] [[sn:hinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]] {{脚注の不足|date=2015年8月12日 (水) 16:00 (UTC)}} [[File:Shinbaru-Nuyama Necropolis 20161014.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Stone Monument|ja|木碑}} of the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group (新原・奴山古墳群)]] [[File:Shimbata-nuyama_kofun.jpg|alt=Distant View [ja] of the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group (新原・奴山古墳群)|thumb]]
'''Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group''' (新原・奴山古墳群) is an assemblage of ancient Kofuns ({{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) that forms one part of the {{ill|Tsuya-zaki Kofun Group|ja|津屋崎古墳群}}, located in the Tsushima–Miyan mountain system of {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}’s {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市}}. It has been designated a National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} (it is included within the Historic Site “{{ill|Tsuya-zaki Kofun Group|ja|津屋崎古墳群}}”). The tumuli were constructed from the latter half of the 5th century to the latter half of the 6th century during the middle-late phase of the {{ill|Kofun Period|ja|古墳時代}}, and those interred are presumed to have been members or affiliates of the {{ill|Munakata Clan|ja|宗像氏}}. The name of the kofun group derives from the fact that it spans the “Shinbara” area (the local name in Katsura) and the “Nuyama” district.
In {{ill|2017|ja|2017年}} ({{ill|Heisei 29|ja|平成29年}}), as one of the constituent assets of the {{ill|'Kami Yadoru Shima' Munakata–Okinoshima Related Heritage Group|ja|「神宿る島」宗像・沖ノ島と関連遺産群}}, the kofun group was inscribed as a {{ill|World Cultural Heritage|ja|世界文化遺産}}. This marks the first instance in Japan in which a group of kofun has been registered as a World Heritage Site.
== Overview == [http://www.okinoshima-heritage.jp/files/Pamphlet_14_file.pdf] occupies a hilly area stretching 800 meters east–west, on which 5 {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Tumuli|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, 1 {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}, and 41 {{ill|Round Tumuli|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} currently survive; these have been assigned sequential numbers for convenience. In addition, historical records, excavation surveys ({{ill|Excavation Survey|ja|発掘調査}}), topographical studies ({{ill|Topography|ja|地形}}) and surveying ({{ill|Surveying|ja|測量}}) have confirmed that another 18 tumuli—lost due to the reclamation of adjacent {{ill|paddy fields|ja|田圃}}—once existed, and these too were assigned sequential numbers.
At the time the tumuli were constructed, an inlet ({{ill|Cove|ja|入り江}}) adjoined the western side of the hill; this inlet, into which the present {{ill|Tsuyazaki Bay|ja|津屋崎湾}} penetrated deeply, gradually receded. During the {{ill|Edo Period|ja|江戸時代}}, the area was reclaimed ({{ill|Land Reclamation|ja|干拓}}) and converted into a {{ill|Salt Field|ja|塩田}}, and from the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治時代}} onward it has been transformed into {{ill|Paddy Fields|ja|水田}}.
Around the kofun group, several {{ill|farm ponds|ja|ため池}}—such as {{ill|Gekka-ike|ja|月花池}}, {{ill|Harada-ike|ja|原田池}}, {{ill|Manekike|ja|招池}}, and {{ill|Fushibara-ike|ja|伏原池}}—are scattered; these were constructed for irrigation purposes during the {{ill|Early Modern Period|ja|近世}} and are unrelated to the kofun. The hilly area is bisected by {{ill|National Route 495|ja|国道495号}}. The basalt ({{ill|Basalt|ja|玄武岩}}) used in the tumuli’s stone chambers is presumed to have been transported by boat from {{ill|Aishima (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|相島 (福岡県)}} in the {{ill|Genkai-nada|ja|玄界灘}}.
== List of Tumuli == * '''Tomb No. 1''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with an estimated total length of 50 meters; the square portion is lost. It was constructed in the first half of the 5th century and is covered with {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}}. * '''Tombs No. 2–6''' – These have not survived due to leveling for road construction and private facility development. * '''Tomb No. 7''' – A square tumulus with sides measuring 20–24 meters; it is the only square tumulus in the {{ill|Munakata Region|ja|宗像地方}}. Constructed in the first half of the 5th century, it yielded an iron ({{ill|Iron|ja|鉄}}) and an axe ({{ill|Axe|ja|斧}}) similar to those found in the ritual remains of {{ill|Okinoshima|ja|沖ノ島}}, suggesting that it may also have served as a ceremonial site. * '''Tombs No. 8–11''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 12''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 43 meters, constructed in the latter half of the 5th century. * '''Tombs No. 13–19''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 20''' – A medium-sized round tumulus covered with fukiishi, constructed in the 5th century. * '''Tomb No. 21''' – A round tumulus with a diameter of 17 meters, constructed in the first half of the 5th century. A stone monument ({{ill|Itaben|ja|板碑}}) from the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property, stands atop its mound. * '''Tomb No. 22''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with an estimated total length of 80 meters, although the square portion is lost. Constructed in the first half of the 5th century, it is the largest in the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group and ranks among the oldest. It was also surrounded by moats and embankments, and a stone shrine of the later-enshrined Nuno-den-gu was erected on its mound. * '''Tomb No. 23''' – A small round tumulus from the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 24''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 53 meters, constructed in the first half of the 5th century, and, along with Tomb No. 22, among the oldest. * '''Tomb No. 25''' – A medium-sized round tumulus covered with fukiishi, constructed in the 5th century. * '''Tombs No. 26–29''' – Small round tumuli built on the slopes of the hill, constructed in the latter half of the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 30''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 54 meters, constructed from the mid to late 5th century. * '''Tombs No. 31–43''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tombs Nos. 44–45''' – These have been lost due to leveling. * '''Tombs Nos. 46–48''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tombs Nos. 49–59''' – These have been lost due to the reclamation of paddy fields.
<gallery perrow="4" widths="175px" heights="175px"> File:新原・奴山古墳群20号墳.JPG|Tomb No. 20 File:新原・奴山古墳群21号墳.JPG|Tomb No. 21 File:新原・奴山古墳群22号墳.JPG|Tomb No. 22 File:新原・奴山古墳群34～36号墳.JPG|Tombs Nos. 34–36 </gallery>
During the selection process for designation, other tumuli—such as the {{ill|Sakurakyo Kofun|ja|桜京古墳}}, {{ill|Tōgō Takatsuka Kofun|ja|東郷高塚古墳}}, and those within the broader {{ill|Tsuya-zaki Kofun Group|ja|津屋崎古墳群}} (which encompasses the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group), including the Katsura Mine-no-Hata Kofun and {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳}}—were omitted from selection. The Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group was retained because its location, facing the sea ({{ill|Sea|ja|海}}) of the {{ill|Genkai-nada|ja|玄界灘}}, preserves a “landscape integrated with the sea” ({{ill|Cultural Landscape|ja|文化的景観}}) that embodies the maritime worship of the {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} (and its associated {{ill|Munakata Sanmeigen|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}), as explicitly stated in the recommendation documents. For these reasons, the more inland {{ill|Sakurakyo Kofun|ja|桜京古墳}} and {{ill|Tōgō Takatsuka Kofun|ja|東郷高塚古墳}} were excluded, and {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳}}—serving as the inner shrine of {{ill|Miyajidake Shrine|ja|宮地嶽神社}} and representing a different aspect of Munakata faith—was also left out.
In the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group, a designated viewscape preservation zone under the {{ill|Landscape Act|ja|景観法}} has been applied to conserve the agricultural lands, rural scenery, and overall landscape surrounding the tumuli—which overlooks {{ill|Oshima (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|大島 (福岡県)}}—and under the {{ill|Outdoor Advertising Law|ja|屋外広告物法}} and local ordinances, incongruous buildings and signboards are periodically removed.<ref>[http://www.city.fukutsu.lg.jp/pdf/kenkou/toshi/keikankeikaku01.pdf]{{ill|Fukutsu City Landscape Plan|ja|福津市景観計画}}</ref>
== Post-World Heritage Registration Developments == Following the heavy rains in {{ill|Heisei 30|ja|平成30年}} July, part of the tumuli collapsed.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20180711185327/https://this.kiji.is/389598562214937697?c=39546741839462401 Damage reported in the World Heritage kofun group, Fukuoka, {{ill|Kyodo News|ja|共同通信}}, July 11, 2018]</ref>
== Access == * From the Miyaji exit of {{ill|Fukuma Station|ja|福間駅}} on the {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}}, take the {{ill|Fukutsu Mini Bus, Katsura Line|ja|ふくつミニバス#勝浦線}} and alight at the Showa Gakuen-mae (Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group) stop. However, only three services operate per day from the station, and only three departures run from the Fukutsu City Complex Cultural Center (Camellia Stage)—the guidance facility for the kofun group—toward Katsura-hama (access to Camellia Stage is made via a connection from Fukuma Station on the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス}} heading to Tsuyazaki). Services do not operate on Sundays, public holidays, or during the New Year period. Because a one-box (minibus) vehicle is used, capacity is limited to 13 people, with priority given to elderly local residents who have voluntarily surrendered their driver's licenses and to those living in areas with insufficient public transport along the route. The fare is a flat rate of 200 yen. Note that the Showa Gakuen-mae (Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group) stop is also the nearest to the Nuyama Observatory, from which one can enjoy a distant view of the kofun group. * By car, it is approximately 13 km from the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}} {{ill|Koga Interchange|ja|古賀インターチェンジ}}.

== See Also == * {{ill|Munakata Clan|ja|宗像氏}} * {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳}} * {{ill|List of Ancient Tombs in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧}}

== List of Tumuli == * '''Tomb No. 1''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with an estimated total length of 50 meters; the square portion is lost. It was constructed in the first half of the 5th century and is covered with {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}}. * '''Tombs No. 2–6''' – These have not survived due to leveling for road construction and private facility development. * '''Tomb No. 7''' – A square tumulus with sides measuring 20–24 meters; it is the only square tumulus in the {{ill|Munakata Region|ja|宗像地方}}. Constructed in the first half of the 5th century, it yielded an iron ({{ill|Iron|ja|鉄}}) and an axe ({{ill|Axe|ja|斧}}) similar to those found in the ritual remains of {{ill|Okinoshima|ja|沖ノ島}}, suggesting that it may also have served as a ceremonial site. * '''Tombs No. 8–11''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 12''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 43 meters, constructed in the latter half of the 5th century. * '''Tombs No. 13–19''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 20''' – A medium-sized round tumulus covered with fukiishi, constructed in the 5th century. * '''Tomb No. 21''' – A round tumulus with a diameter of 17 meters, constructed in the first half of the 5th century. A stone monument ({{ill|Itaben|ja|板碑}}) from the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property, stands atop its mound. * '''Tomb No. 22''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with an estimated total length of 80 meters, although the square portion is lost. Constructed in the first half of the 5th century, it is the largest in the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group and ranks among the oldest. It was also surrounded by moats and embankments, and a stone shrine of the later-enshrined Nuno-den-gu was erected on its mound. * '''Tomb No. 23''' – A small round tumulus from the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 24''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 53 meters, constructed in the first half of the 5th century, and, along with Tomb No. 22, among the oldest. * '''Tomb No. 25''' – A medium-sized round tumulus covered with fukiishi, constructed in the 5th century. * '''Tombs No. 26–29''' – Small round tumuli built on the slopes of the hill, constructed in the latter half of the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 30''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 54 meters, constructed from the mid to late 5th century. * '''Tombs No. 31–43''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tombs Nos. 44–45''' – These have been lost due to leveling. * '''Tombs Nos. 46–48''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tombs Nos. 49–59''' – These have been lost due to the reclamation of paddy fields.
<gallery perrow="4" widths="175px" heights="175px"> File:新原・奴山古墳群20号墳.JPG|Tomb No. 20 File:新原・奴山古墳群21号墳.JPG|Tomb No. 21 File:新原・奴山古墳群22号墳.JPG|Tomb No. 22 File:新原・奴山古墳群34～36号墳.JPG|Tombs Nos. 34–36 </gallery>
During the selection process for designation, other tumuli—such as the {{ill|Sakurakyo Kofun|ja|桜京古墳}}, {{ill|Tōgō Takatsuka Kofun|ja|東郷高塚古墳}}, and those within the broader {{ill|Tsuya-zaki Kofun Group|ja|津屋崎古墳群}} (which encompasses the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group), including the Katsura Mine-no-Hata Kofun and {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳}}—were omitted from selection. The Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group was retained because its location, facing the sea ({{ill|Sea|ja|海}}) of the {{ill|Genkai-nada|ja|玄界灘}}, preserves a “landscape integrated with the sea” ({{ill|Cultural Landscape|ja|文化的景観}}) that embodies the maritime worship of the {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} (and its associated {{ill|Munakata Sanmeigen|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}), as explicitly stated in the recommendation documents. For these reasons, the more inland {{ill|Sakurakyo Kofun|ja|桜京古墳}} and {{ill|Tōgō Takatsuka Kofun|ja|東郷高塚古墳}} were excluded, and {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳}}—serving as the inner shrine of {{ill|Miyajidake Shrine|ja|宮地嶽神社}} and representing a different aspect of Munakata faith—was also left out.
In the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group, a designated viewscape preservation zone under the {{ill|Landscape Act|ja|景観法}} has been applied to conserve the agricultural lands, rural scenery, and overall landscape surrounding the tumuli—which overlooks {{ill|Oshima (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|大島 (福岡県)}}—and under the {{ill|Outdoor Advertising Law|ja|屋外広告物法}} and local ordinances, incongruous buildings and signboards are periodically removed.<ref>[http://www.city.fukutsu.lg.jp/pdf/kenkou/toshi/keikankeikaku01.pdf]{{ill|Fukutsu City Landscape Plan|ja|福津市景観計画}}</ref>
== Post-World Heritage Registration Developments == Following the heavy rains in {{ill|Heisei 30|ja|平成30年}} July, part of the tumuli collapsed.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20180711185327/https://this.kiji.is/389598562214937697?c=39546741839462401 Damage reported in the World Heritage kofun group, Fukuoka, {{ill|Kyodo News|ja|共同通信}}, July 11, 2018]</ref>
== Access == * From the Miyaji exit of {{ill|Fukuma Station|ja|福間駅}} on the {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}}, take the {{ill|Fukutsu Mini Bus, Katsura Line|ja|ふくつミニバス#勝浦線}} and alight at the Showa Gakuen-mae (Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group) stop. However, only three services operate per day from the station, and only three departures run from the Fukutsu City Complex Cultural Center (Camellia Stage)—the guidance facility for the kofun group—toward Katsura-hama (access to Camellia Stage is made via a connection from Fukuma Station on the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス}} heading to Tsuyazaki). Services do not operate on Sundays, public holidays, or during the New Year period. Because a one-box (minibus) vehicle is used, capacity is limited to 13 people, with priority given to elderly local residents who have voluntarily surrendered their driver's licenses and to those living in areas with insufficient public transport along the route. The fare is a flat rate of 200 yen. Note that the Showa Gakuen-mae (Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group) stop is also the nearest to the Nuyama Observatory, from which one can enjoy a distant view of the kofun group. * By car, it is approximately 13 km from the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}} {{ill|Koga Interchange|ja|古賀インターチェンジ}}.

{{DEFAULTSORT:しんはる ぬやまこふんくん}} [[Category:Ancient Tombs in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:ill|'Kami Yadoru Shima' Munakata–Okinoshima Related Heritage Group|ja|「神宿る島」宗像・沖ノ島と関連遺産群}}]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites of Tombs]] [[Category:History of Fukutsu City]] [[Category:Kofun Groups]] [[ja:新原・奴山古墳群]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[sn:hinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Pages to be translated]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]] [[sn:hinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:Pages to be translated]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[sn:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:円墳]][[sn:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:円墳]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:円墳]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:円墳]] [[sn:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[sn:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]][[sn:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]] [[Category:c]][[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:C tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[en:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]]

[[ja:%E6%96%B0%E5%8E%9F%E3%83%BB%E5%A5%B4%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[Category:Shortest 250 pages as of 2025-02-19]] [[sn:hinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]] {{脚注の不足|date=2015年8月12日 (水) 16:00 (UTC)}} [[File:Shinbaru-Nuyama Necropolis 20161014.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Stone Monument|ja|木碑}} of the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group (新原・奴山古墳群)]] [[File:Shimbata-nuyama_kofun.jpg|alt=Distant View [ja] of the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group (新原・奴山古墳群)|thumb]]
'''Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group''' (新原・奴山古墳群) is an assemblage of ancient Kofuns ({{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) that forms one part of the {{ill|Tsuya-zaki Kofun Group|ja|津屋崎古墳群}}, located in the Tsushima–Miyan mountain system of {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}’s {{ill|Fukutsu City|ja|福津市}}. It has been designated a National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} (it is included within the Historic Site “{{ill|Tsuya-zaki Kofun Group|ja|津屋崎古墳群}}”). The tumuli were constructed from the latter half of the 5th century to the latter half of the 6th century during the middle-late phase of the {{ill|Kofun Period|ja|古墳時代}}, and those interred are presumed to have been members or affiliates of the {{ill|Munakata Clan|ja|宗像氏}}. The name of the kofun group derives from the fact that it spans the “Shinbara” area (the local name in Katsura) and the “Nuyama” district.
In {{ill|2017|ja|2017年}} ({{ill|Heisei 29|ja|平成29年}}), as one of the constituent assets of the {{ill|'Kami Yadoru Shima' Munakata–Okinoshima Related Heritage Group|ja|「神宿る島」宗像・沖ノ島と関連遺産群}}, the kofun group was inscribed as a {{ill|World Cultural Heritage|ja|世界文化遺産}}. This marks the first instance in Japan in which a group of kofun has been registered as a World Heritage Site.
== Overview == [http://www.okinoshima-heritage.jp/files/Pamphlet_14_file.pdf] occupies a hilly area stretching 800 meters east–west, on which 5 {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Tumuli|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, 1 {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}, and 41 {{ill|Round Tumuli|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} currently survive; these have been assigned sequential numbers for convenience. In addition, historical records, excavation surveys ({{ill|Excavation Survey|ja|発掘調査}}), topographical studies ({{ill|Topography|ja|地形}}) and surveying ({{ill|Surveying|ja|測量}}) have confirmed that another 18 tumuli—lost due to the reclamation of adjacent {{ill|paddy fields|ja|田圃}}—once existed, and these too were assigned sequential numbers.
At the time the tumuli were constructed, an inlet ({{ill|Cove|ja|入り江}}) adjoined the western side of the hill; this inlet, into which the present {{ill|Tsuyazaki Bay|ja|津屋崎湾}} penetrated deeply, gradually receded. During the {{ill|Edo Period|ja|江戸時代}}, the area was reclaimed ({{ill|Land Reclamation|ja|干拓}}) and converted into a {{ill|Salt Field|ja|塩田}}, and from the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治時代}} onward it has been transformed into {{ill|Paddy Fields|ja|水田}}.
Around the kofun group, several {{ill|farm ponds|ja|ため池}}—such as {{ill|Gekka-ike|ja|月花池}}, {{ill|Harada-ike|ja|原田池}}, {{ill|Manekike|ja|招池}}, and {{ill|Fushibara-ike|ja|伏原池}}—are scattered; these were constructed for irrigation purposes during the {{ill|Early Modern Period|ja|近世}} and are unrelated to the kofun. The hilly area is bisected by {{ill|National Route 495|ja|国道495号}}. The basalt ({{ill|Basalt|ja|玄武岩}}) used in the tumuli’s stone chambers is presumed to have been transported by boat from {{ill|Aishima (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|相島 (福岡県)}} in the {{ill|Genkai-nada|ja|玄界灘}}.
