{{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Shinbaru-Nuyama Necropolis 20161014.jpg|location={{ill|Fukutsu|qid=Q825700}}|coordinates={{Coord|33.8175|N|130.48583333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|builder={{ill|Munakata clan|qid=Q16481907}}|part_of={{ill|Sacred Island of Okinoshima and Associated Sites in the Munakata Region|qid=Q324638}}; {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Group|qid=Q109595331}}|Historic Site={{ill|part of UNESCO World Heritage Site|qid=Q43113623}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q22119078]] Wikidata name: Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:27, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Add wikidata]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{More footnotes needed|date=2015th year 8月12日 (水) 16:00 (UTC)}} [[File:Shinbaru-Nuyama Necropolis 20161014.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Stone Monument|ja|木碑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Monument|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group (新原・奴山古墳群)]] [[File:Shimbata-nuyama_kofun.jpg|alt=Distant View [ja] of the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group (新原・奴山古墳群)|thumb]]
'''Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group''' (新原・奴山古墳群) is an assemblage of ancient Kofuns ([[:en:Kofun|Kofun]]) that forms one part of the [[:en:Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|Tsuya-zaki Kofun Group]], located in the Tsushima–Miyan mountain system of [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]’s [[:en:Fukutsu, Fukuoka|Fukutsu City]]. It has been designated a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} (it is included within the Historic Site “[[:en:Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|Tsuya-zaki Kofun Group]]”). The tumuli were constructed from the latter half of the 5th century to the latter half of the 6th century during the middle-late phase of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun Period]], and those interred are presumed to have been members or affiliates of the {{ill|Munakata Clan|ja|宗像氏|1=Munakata clan|fr|Clan Munakata|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|qq=}}. The name of the kofun group derives from the fact that it spans the “Shinbara” area (the local name in Katsura) and the “Nuyama” district.
In [[:en:2017|2017]] ([[:en:2017|Heisei 29]]), as one of the constituent assets of the [[:en:Sacred Island of Okinoshima and Associated Sites in the Munakata Region|Sacred Island of Okinoshima and Associated Sites in the Munakata Region]], the kofun group was inscribed as a {{ill|World Cultural Heritage|ja|世界文化遺産|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/World Cultural Heritage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 文化遺産 (世界遺産)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 文化遺産 (世界遺産)|1=cultural heritage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q210272}}. This marks the first instance in Japan in which a group of kofun has been registered as a World Heritage Site.
== Overview == [http://www.okinoshima-heritage.jp/files/Pamphlet_14_file.pdf] occupies a hilly area stretching 800 meters east–west, on which 5 [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], 1 {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and 41 {{ill|Round Tumuli|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Tumuli|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} currently survive; these have been assigned sequential numbers for convenience. In addition, historical records, excavation surveys ([[:en:Archaeological excavation|Excavation Survey]]), topographical studies ([[:en:Landform|Topography]]) and surveying ([[:en:Surveying|Surveying]]) have confirmed that another 18 tumuli—lost due to the reclamation of adjacent {{ill|paddy fields|ja|田圃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/paddy fields|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田|1=paddy field|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q842623}}—once existed, and these too were assigned sequential numbers.
At the time the tumuli were constructed, an inlet ([[:en:Inlet|Cove]]) adjoined the western side of the hill; this inlet, into which the present {{ill|Tsuyazaki Bay|ja|津屋崎湾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuyazaki Bay|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} penetrated deeply, gradually receded. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo Period]], the area was reclaimed ([[:en:Polder|Land Reclamation]]) and converted into a [[:en:Salt evaporation pond|Salt Field]], and from the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] onward it has been transformed into {{ill|Paddy Fields|ja|水田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Paddy Fields|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田|1=paddy field|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q842623}}.
Around the kofun group, several {{ill|farm ponds|ja|ため池|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farm ponds|qq=|1=irrigation reservoir|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q112083751}}—such as {{ill|Gekka-ike|ja|月花池|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekka-ike|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Harada-ike|ja|原田池|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harada-ike|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Manekike|ja|招池|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manekike|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Fushibara-ike|ja|伏原池|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushibara-ike|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—are scattered; these were constructed for irrigation purposes during the [[:en:Early modern period|Early Modern Period]] and are unrelated to the kofun. The hilly area is bisected by [[:en:Japan National Route 495|National Route 495]]. The basalt ([[:en:Basalt|Basalt]]) used in the tumuli’s stone chambers is presumed to have been transported by boat from [[:en:Ainoshima (Shingū)|Aishima (Fukuoka Prefecture)]] in the [[:en:Genkai Sea|Genkai-nada]].
== List of Tumuli == * '''Tomb No. 1''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with an estimated total length of 50 meters; the square portion is lost. It was constructed in the first half of the 5th century and is covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]]. * '''Tombs No. 2–6''' – These have not survived due to leveling for road construction and private facility development. * '''Tomb No. 7''' – A square tumulus with sides measuring 20–24 meters; it is the only square tumulus in the {{ill|Munakata Region|ja|宗像地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Region|qq=|1=Munakata region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451849}}. Constructed in the first half of the 5th century, it yielded an iron ([[:en:Iron|Iron]]) and an axe ([[:en:Axe|Axe]]) similar to those found in the ritual remains of [[:en:Okinoshima (Fukuoka)|Okinoshima]], suggesting that it may also have served as a ceremonial site. * '''Tombs No. 8–11''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 12''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 43 meters, constructed in the latter half of the 5th century. * '''Tombs No. 13–19''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 20''' – A medium-sized round tumulus covered with fukiishi, constructed in the 5th century. * '''Tomb No. 21''' – A round tumulus with a diameter of 17 meters, constructed in the first half of the 5th century. A stone monument ({{ill|Itaben|ja|板碑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itaben|qq=|1=itabi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11532699}}) from the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura Period]], designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property, stands atop its mound. * '''Tomb No. 22''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with an estimated total length of 80 meters, although the square portion is lost. Constructed in the first half of the 5th century, it is the largest in the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group and Ranks among the oldest. It was also surrounded by moats and embankments, and a stone shrine of the later-enshrined Nuno-den-gu was erected on its mound. * '''Tomb No. 23''' – A small round tumulus from the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 24''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 53 meters, constructed in the first half of the 5th century, and, along with Tomb No. 22, among the oldest. * '''Tomb No. 25''' – A medium-sized round tumulus covered with fukiishi, constructed in the 5th century. * '''Tombs No. 26–29''' – Small round tumuli built on the slopes of the hill, constructed in the latter half of the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 30''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 54 meters, constructed from the mid to late 5th century. * '''Tombs No. 31–43''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tombs Nos. 44–45''' – These have been lost due to leveling. * '''Tombs Nos. 46–48''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tombs Nos. 49–59''' – These have been lost due to the reclamation of paddy fields.
<gallery perrow="4" widths="175px" heights="175px"> File:新原・奴山古墳群20号墳.JPG|Tomb No. 20 File:新原・奴山古墳群21号墳.JPG|Tomb No. 21 File:新原・奴山古墳群22号墳.JPG|Tomb No. 22 File:新原・奴山古墳群34～36号墳.JPG|Tombs Nos. 34–36 </gallery>
During the selection process for designation, other tumuli—such as the [[:en:Sakurakyō Kofun|Sakurakyo Kofun]], {{ill|Tōgō Takatsuka Kofun|ja|東郷高塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōgō Takatsuka Kofun|qq=|1=Tōgō Takatsuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24867823}}, and those within the broader [[:en:Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|Tsuya-zaki Kofun Group]] (which encompasses the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group), including the Katsura Mine-no-Hata Kofun and {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyajidake Kofun|qq=}}—were omitted from selection. The Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group was retained because its location, facing the sea ([[:en:Sea|Sea]]) of the [[:en:Genkai Sea|Genkai-nada]], preserves a “landscape integrated with the sea” ([[:en:Cultural landscape|Cultural Landscape]]) that embodies the maritime worship of the [[:en:Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]] (and its associated {{ill|Munakata Sanmeigen|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanmeigen|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}), as explicitly stated in the recommendation documents. For these reasons, the more inland [[:en:Sakurakyō Kofun|Sakurakyo Kofun]] and {{ill|Tōgō Takatsuka Kofun|ja|東郷高塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōgō Takatsuka Kofun|qq=|1=Tōgō Takatsuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24867823}} were excluded, and {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyajidake Kofun|qq=}}—serving as the inner shrine of [[:en:Miyajidake Shrine|Miyajidake Shrine]] and representing a different aspect of Munakata faith—was also left out.
In the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group, a designated viewscape preservation zone under the {{ill|Landscape Act|ja|景観法|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Landscape Act|qq=|1=Landscape Act|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11514692}} has been applied to conserve the agricultural lands, rural scenery, and overall landscape surrounding the tumuli—which overlooks {{ill|Oshima (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|大島 (福岡県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshima (Fukuoka Prefecture)|qq=|1=Ōshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434813}}—and under the {{ill|Outdoor Advertising Law|ja|屋外広告物法|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outdoor Advertising Law|qq=|1=Outdoor Advertisement Act|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465670}} and local ordinances, incongruous buildings and signboards are periodically removed.<ref>[http://www.city.fukutsu.lg.jp/pdf/kenkou/toshi/keikankeikaku01.pdf]{{ill|Fukutsu City Landscape Plan|ja|福津市景観計画|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukutsu City Landscape Plan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref>
== Post-World Heritage Registration Developments == Following the heavy rains in [[:en:2018|Heisei 30]] July, part of the tumuli collapsed.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20180711185327/https://this.kiji.is/389598562214937697?c=39546741839462401 Damage reported in the World Heritage kofun group, Fukuoka, {{ill|Kyodo News|ja|共同通信|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyodo News|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 共同通信社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 共同通信社|1=Kyodo News|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q779730}}, July 11, 2018]</ref>
== Access == * From the Miyaji exit of [[:en:Fukuma Station|Fukuma Station]] on the [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]], take the {{ill|Fukutsu Mini Bus, Katsura Line|ja|ふくつミニバス#勝浦線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukutsu Mini Bus, Katsura Line|qq=|1=Fukutsu Mini Bus, Katsura Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11276990}} and alight at the Showa Gakuen-mae (Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group) stop. However, only three services operate per day from the station, and only three departures run from the Fukutsu City Complex Cultural Center (Camellia Stage)—the guidance facility for the kofun group—toward Katsura-hama (access to Camellia Stage is made via a connection from Fukuma Station on the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|qq=|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} heading to Tsuyazaki). Services do not operate on Sundays, public holidays, or during the New Year period. Because a one-box (minibus) vehicle is used, capacity is limited to 13 people, with priority given to elderly local residents who have voluntarily surrendered their driver's licenses and to those living in areas with insufficient public transport along the route. The fare is a flat rate of 200 yen. Note that the Showa Gakuen-mae (Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group) stop is also the nearest to the Nuyama Observatory, from which one can enjoy a distant view of the kofun group. * By car, it is approximately 13 km from the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]] {{ill|Koga Interchange|ja|古賀インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koga Interchange|qq=|1=Koga interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1577981}}.
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Munakata Clan|ja|宗像氏|1=Munakata clan|fr|Clan Munakata|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyajidake Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Ancient Tombs in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ancient Tombs in Japan|qq=|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|qq=|1=List of historic sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370155}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Shinbaru-Nuyama Necropolis 20161014.jpg|location={{ill|Fukutsu|qid=Q825700}}|coordinates={{Coord|33.8175|N|130.48583333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|builder={{ill|Munakata clan|qid=Q16481907}}|part_of={{ill|Sacred Island of Okinoshima and Associated Sites in the Munakata Region|qid=Q324638}}; {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Group|qid=Q109595331}}|Historic Site={{ill|part of UNESCO World Heritage Site|qid=Q43113623}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q22119078]] Wikidata name: Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:27, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Add wikidata]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{More footnotes needed|date=2015th year 8月12日 (水) 16:00 (UTC)}} [[File:Shinbaru-Nuyama Necropolis 20161014.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Stone Monument|ja|木碑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Monument|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group (新原・奴山古墳群)]] [[File:Shimbata-nuyama_kofun.jpg|alt=Distant View [ja] of the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group (新原・奴山古墳群)|thumb]]
'''Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group''' (新原・奴山古墳群) is an assemblage of ancient Kofuns ([[:en:Kofun|Kofun]]) that forms one part of the [[:en:Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|Tsuya-zaki Kofun Group]], located in the Tsushima–Miyan mountain system of [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]’s [[:en:Fukutsu, Fukuoka|Fukutsu City]]. It has been designated a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} (it is included within the Historic Site “[[:en:Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|Tsuya-zaki Kofun Group]]”). The tumuli were constructed from the latter half of the 5th century to the latter half of the 6th century during the middle-late phase of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun Period]], and those interred are presumed to have been members or affiliates of the {{ill|Munakata Clan|ja|宗像氏|1=Munakata clan|fr|Clan Munakata|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|qq=}}. The name of the kofun group derives from the fact that it spans the “Shinbara” area (the local name in Katsura) and the “Nuyama” district.
In [[:en:2017|2017]] ([[:en:2017|Heisei 29]]), as one of the constituent assets of the [[:en:Sacred Island of Okinoshima and Associated Sites in the Munakata Region|Sacred Island of Okinoshima and Associated Sites in the Munakata Region]], the kofun group was inscribed as a {{ill|World Cultural Heritage|ja|世界文化遺産|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/World Cultural Heritage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 文化遺産 (世界遺産)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 文化遺産 (世界遺産)|1=cultural heritage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q210272}}. This marks the first instance in Japan in which a group of kofun has been registered as a World Heritage Site.
== Overview == [http://www.okinoshima-heritage.jp/files/Pamphlet_14_file.pdf] occupies a hilly area stretching 800 meters east–west, on which 5 [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], 1 {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and 41 {{ill|Round Tumuli|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Tumuli|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} currently survive; these have been assigned sequential numbers for convenience. In addition, historical records, excavation surveys ([[:en:Archaeological excavation|Excavation Survey]]), topographical studies ([[:en:Landform|Topography]]) and surveying ([[:en:Surveying|Surveying]]) have confirmed that another 18 tumuli—lost due to the reclamation of adjacent {{ill|paddy fields|ja|田圃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/paddy fields|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田|1=paddy field|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q842623}}—once existed, and these too were assigned sequential numbers.
At the time the tumuli were constructed, an inlet ([[:en:Inlet|Cove]]) adjoined the western side of the hill; this inlet, into which the present {{ill|Tsuyazaki Bay|ja|津屋崎湾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuyazaki Bay|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} penetrated deeply, gradually receded. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo Period]], the area was reclaimed ([[:en:Polder|Land Reclamation]]) and converted into a [[:en:Salt evaporation pond|Salt Field]], and from the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] onward it has been transformed into {{ill|Paddy Fields|ja|水田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Paddy Fields|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田|1=paddy field|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q842623}}.
Around the kofun group, several {{ill|farm ponds|ja|ため池|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farm ponds|qq=|1=irrigation reservoir|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q112083751}}—such as {{ill|Gekka-ike|ja|月花池|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekka-ike|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Harada-ike|ja|原田池|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harada-ike|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Manekike|ja|招池|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manekike|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Fushibara-ike|ja|伏原池|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushibara-ike|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—are scattered; these were constructed for irrigation purposes during the [[:en:Early modern period|Early Modern Period]] and are unrelated to the kofun. The hilly area is bisected by [[:en:Japan National Route 495|National Route 495]]. The basalt ([[:en:Basalt|Basalt]]) used in the tumuli’s stone chambers is presumed to have been transported by boat from [[:en:Ainoshima (Shingū)|Aishima (Fukuoka Prefecture)]] in the [[:en:Genkai Sea|Genkai-nada]].
== List of Tumuli == * '''Tomb No. 1''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with an estimated total length of 50 meters; the square portion is lost. It was constructed in the first half of the 5th century and is covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]]. * '''Tombs No. 2–6''' – These have not survived due to leveling for road construction and private facility development. * '''Tomb No. 7''' – A square tumulus with sides measuring 20–24 meters; it is the only square tumulus in the {{ill|Munakata Region|ja|宗像地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Region|qq=|1=Munakata region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451849}}. Constructed in the first half of the 5th century, it yielded an iron ([[:en:Iron|Iron]]) and an axe ([[:en:Axe|Axe]]) similar to those found in the ritual remains of [[:en:Okinoshima (Fukuoka)|Okinoshima]], suggesting that it may also have served as a ceremonial site. * '''Tombs No. 8–11''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 12''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 43 meters, constructed in the latter half of the 5th century. * '''Tombs No. 13–19''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 20''' – A medium-sized round tumulus covered with fukiishi, constructed in the 5th century. * '''Tomb No. 21''' – A round tumulus with a diameter of 17 meters, constructed in the first half of the 5th century. A stone monument ({{ill|Itaben|ja|板碑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itaben|qq=|1=itabi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11532699}}) from the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura Period]], designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property, stands atop its mound. * '''Tomb No. 22''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with an estimated total length of 80 meters, although the square portion is lost. Constructed in the first half of the 5th century, it is the largest in the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group and Ranks among the oldest. It was also surrounded by moats and embankments, and a stone shrine of the later-enshrined Nuno-den-gu was erected on its mound. * '''Tomb No. 23''' – A small round tumulus from the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 24''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 53 meters, constructed in the first half of the 5th century, and, along with Tomb No. 22, among the oldest. * '''Tomb No. 25''' – A medium-sized round tumulus covered with fukiishi, constructed in the 5th century. * '''Tombs No. 26–29''' – Small round tumuli built on the slopes of the hill, constructed in the latter half of the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 30''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 54 meters, constructed from the mid to late 5th century. * '''Tombs No. 31–43''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tombs Nos. 44–45''' – These have been lost due to leveling. * '''Tombs Nos. 46–48''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tombs Nos. 49–59''' – These have been lost due to the reclamation of paddy fields.
<gallery perrow="4" widths="175px" heights="175px"> File:新原・奴山古墳群20号墳.JPG|Tomb No. 20 File:新原・奴山古墳群21号墳.JPG|Tomb No. 21 File:新原・奴山古墳群22号墳.JPG|Tomb No. 22 File:新原・奴山古墳群34～36号墳.JPG|Tombs Nos. 34–36 </gallery>
During the selection process for designation, other tumuli—such as the [[:en:Sakurakyō Kofun|Sakurakyo Kofun]], {{ill|Tōgō Takatsuka Kofun|ja|東郷高塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōgō Takatsuka Kofun|qq=|1=Tōgō Takatsuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24867823}}, and those within the broader [[:en:Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|Tsuya-zaki Kofun Group]] (which encompasses the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group), including the Katsura Mine-no-Hata Kofun and {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyajidake Kofun|qq=}}—were omitted from selection. The Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group was retained because its location, facing the sea ([[:en:Sea|Sea]]) of the [[:en:Genkai Sea|Genkai-nada]], preserves a “landscape integrated with the sea” ([[:en:Cultural landscape|Cultural Landscape]]) that embodies the maritime worship of the [[:en:Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]] (and its associated {{ill|Munakata Sanmeigen|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanmeigen|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}), as explicitly stated in the recommendation documents. For these reasons, the more inland [[:en:Sakurakyō Kofun|Sakurakyo Kofun]] and {{ill|Tōgō Takatsuka Kofun|ja|東郷高塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōgō Takatsuka Kofun|qq=|1=Tōgō Takatsuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24867823}} were excluded, and {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyajidake Kofun|qq=}}—serving as the inner shrine of [[:en:Miyajidake Shrine|Miyajidake Shrine]] and representing a different aspect of Munakata faith—was also left out.
In the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group, a designated viewscape preservation zone under the {{ill|Landscape Act|ja|景観法|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Landscape Act|qq=|1=Landscape Act|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11514692}} has been applied to conserve the agricultural lands, rural scenery, and overall landscape surrounding the tumuli—which overlooks {{ill|Oshima (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|大島 (福岡県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshima (Fukuoka Prefecture)|qq=|1=Ōshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434813}}—and under the {{ill|Outdoor Advertising Law|ja|屋外広告物法|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outdoor Advertising Law|qq=|1=Outdoor Advertisement Act|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465670}} and local ordinances, incongruous buildings and signboards are periodically removed.<ref>[http://www.city.fukutsu.lg.jp/pdf/kenkou/toshi/keikankeikaku01.pdf]{{ill|Fukutsu City Landscape Plan|ja|福津市景観計画|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukutsu City Landscape Plan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref>
== Post-World Heritage Registration Developments == Following the heavy rains in [[:en:2018|Heisei 30]] July, part of the tumuli collapsed.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20180711185327/https://this.kiji.is/389598562214937697?c=39546741839462401 Damage reported in the World Heritage kofun group, Fukuoka, {{ill|Kyodo News|ja|共同通信|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyodo News|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 共同通信社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 共同通信社|1=Kyodo News|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q779730}}, July 11, 2018]</ref>
== Access == * From the Miyaji exit of [[:en:Fukuma Station|Fukuma Station]] on the [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]], take the {{ill|Fukutsu Mini Bus, Katsura Line|ja|ふくつミニバス#勝浦線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukutsu Mini Bus, Katsura Line|qq=|1=Fukutsu Mini Bus, Katsura Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11276990}} and alight at the Showa Gakuen-mae (Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group) stop. However, only three services operate per day from the station, and only three departures run from the Fukutsu City Complex Cultural Center (Camellia Stage)—the guidance facility for the kofun group—toward Katsura-hama (access to Camellia Stage is made via a connection from Fukuma Station on the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|qq=|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} heading to Tsuyazaki). Services do not operate on Sundays, public holidays, or during the New Year period. Because a one-box (minibus) vehicle is used, capacity is limited to 13 people, with priority given to elderly local residents who have voluntarily surrendered their driver's licenses and to those living in areas with insufficient public transport along the route. The fare is a flat rate of 200 yen. Note that the Showa Gakuen-mae (Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group) stop is also the nearest to the Nuyama Observatory, from which one can enjoy a distant view of the kofun group. * By car, it is approximately 13 km from the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]] {{ill|Koga Interchange|ja|古賀インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koga Interchange|qq=|1=Koga interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1577981}}.

[[sn:E6%96%B0%E5%8E%9F%E3%83%BB%E5%A5%B4%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[sn:hinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]]
[[ja:新原・奴山古墳群]] [[sn:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Sacred Island of Okinoshima and Associated Sites in the Munakata Region]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Cemeteries on the World Heritage List]]
[[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Fukutsu]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group}}

{{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Shinbaru-Nuyama Necropolis 20161014.jpg|location={{ill|Fukutsu|qid=Q825700}}|coordinates={{Coord|33.8175|N|130.48583333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|builder={{ill|Munakata clan|qid=Q16481907}}|part_of={{ill|Sacred Island of Okinoshima and Associated Sites in the Munakata Region|qid=Q324638}}; {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Group|qid=Q109595331}}|Historic Site={{ill|part of UNESCO World Heritage Site|qid=Q43113623}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q22119078]] Wikidata name: Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:27, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{More footnotes needed|date=2015th year 8月12日 (水) 16:00 (UTC)}} [[File:Shinbaru-Nuyama Necropolis 20161014.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Stone Monument|ja|木碑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Monument|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group (新原・奴山古墳群)]] [[File:Shimbata-nuyama_kofun.jpg|alt=Distant View [ja] of the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group (新原・奴山古墳群)|thumb]]
'''Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group''' (新原・奴山古墳群) is an assemblage of ancient Kofuns ([[:en:Kofun|Kofun]]) that forms one part of the [[:en:Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|Tsuya-zaki Kofun Group]], located in the Tsushima–Miyan mountain system of [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]’s [[:en:Fukutsu, Fukuoka|Fukutsu City]]. It has been designated a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} (it is included within the Historic Site “[[:en:Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|Tsuya-zaki Kofun Group]]”). The tumuli were constructed from the latter half of the 5th century to the latter half of the 6th century during the middle-late phase of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun Period]], and those interred are presumed to have been members or affiliates of the {{ill|Munakata Clan|ja|宗像氏|1=Munakata clan|fr|Clan Munakata|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|qq=}}. The name of the kofun group derives from the fact that it spans the “Shinbara” area (the local name in Katsura) and the “Nuyama” district.
In [[:en:2017|2017]] ([[:en:2017|Heisei 29]]), as one of the constituent assets of the [[:en:Sacred Island of Okinoshima and Associated Sites in the Munakata Region|Sacred Island of Okinoshima and Associated Sites in the Munakata Region]], the kofun group was inscribed as a {{ill|World Cultural Heritage|ja|世界文化遺産|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/World Cultural Heritage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 文化遺産 (世界遺産)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 文化遺産 (世界遺産)|1=cultural heritage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q210272}}. This marks the first instance in Japan in which a group of kofun has been registered as a World Heritage Site.
== Overview == [http://www.okinoshima-heritage.jp/files/Pamphlet_14_file.pdf] occupies a hilly area stretching 800 meters east–west, on which 5 [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], 1 {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and 41 {{ill|Round Tumuli|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Tumuli|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} currently survive; these have been assigned sequential numbers for convenience. In addition, historical records, excavation surveys ([[:en:Archaeological excavation|Excavation Survey]]), topographical studies ([[:en:Landform|Topography]]) and surveying ([[:en:Surveying|Surveying]]) have confirmed that another 18 tumuli—lost due to the reclamation of adjacent {{ill|paddy fields|ja|田圃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/paddy fields|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田|1=paddy field|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q842623}}—once existed, and these too were assigned sequential numbers.
At the time the tumuli were constructed, an inlet ([[:en:Inlet|Cove]]) adjoined the western side of the hill; this inlet, into which the present {{ill|Tsuyazaki Bay|ja|津屋崎湾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuyazaki Bay|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} penetrated deeply, gradually receded. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo Period]], the area was reclaimed ([[:en:Polder|Land Reclamation]]) and converted into a [[:en:Salt evaporation pond|Salt Field]], and from the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] onward it has been transformed into {{ill|Paddy Fields|ja|水田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Paddy Fields|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田|1=paddy field|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q842623}}.
Around the kofun group, several {{ill|farm ponds|ja|ため池|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farm ponds|qq=|1=irrigation reservoir|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q112083751}}—such as {{ill|Gekka-ike|ja|月花池|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekka-ike|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Harada-ike|ja|原田池|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harada-ike|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Manekike|ja|招池|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manekike|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Fushibara-ike|ja|伏原池|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushibara-ike|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—are scattered; these were constructed for irrigation purposes during the [[:en:Early modern period|Early Modern Period]] and are unrelated to the kofun. The hilly area is bisected by [[:en:Japan National Route 495|National Route 495]]. The basalt ([[:en:Basalt|Basalt]]) used in the tumuli’s stone chambers is presumed to have been transported by boat from [[:en:Ainoshima (Shingū)|Aishima (Fukuoka Prefecture)]] in the [[:en:Genkai Sea|Genkai-nada]].
== List of Tumuli == * '''Tomb No. 1''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with an estimated total length of 50 meters; the square portion is lost. It was constructed in the first half of the 5th century and is covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]]. * '''Tombs No. 2–6''' – These have not survived due to leveling for road construction and private facility development. * '''Tomb No. 7''' – A square tumulus with sides measuring 20–24 meters; it is the only square tumulus in the {{ill|Munakata Region|ja|宗像地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Region|qq=|1=Munakata region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451849}}. Constructed in the first half of the 5th century, it yielded an iron ([[:en:Iron|Iron]]) and an axe ([[:en:Axe|Axe]]) similar to those found in the ritual remains of [[:en:Okinoshima (Fukuoka)|Okinoshima]], suggesting that it may also have served as a ceremonial site. * '''Tombs No. 8–11''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 12''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 43 meters, constructed in the latter half of the 5th century. * '''Tombs No. 13–19''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 20''' – A medium-sized round tumulus covered with fukiishi, constructed in the 5th century. * '''Tomb No. 21''' – A round tumulus with a diameter of 17 meters, constructed in the first half of the 5th century. A stone monument ({{ill|Itaben|ja|板碑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itaben|qq=|1=itabi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11532699}}) from the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura Period]], designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property, stands atop its mound. * '''Tomb No. 22''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with an estimated total length of 80 meters, although the square portion is lost. Constructed in the first half of the 5th century, it is the largest in the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group and Ranks among the oldest. It was also surrounded by moats and embankments, and a stone shrine of the later-enshrined Nuno-den-gu was erected on its mound. * '''Tomb No. 23''' – A small round tumulus from the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 24''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 53 meters, constructed in the first half of the 5th century, and, along with Tomb No. 22, among the oldest. * '''Tomb No. 25''' – A medium-sized round tumulus covered with fukiishi, constructed in the 5th century. * '''Tombs No. 26–29''' – Small round tumuli built on the slopes of the hill, constructed in the latter half of the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 30''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 54 meters, constructed from the mid to late 5th century. * '''Tombs No. 31–43''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tombs Nos. 44–45''' – These have been lost due to leveling. * '''Tombs Nos. 46–48''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tombs Nos. 49–59''' – These have been lost due to the reclamation of paddy fields.
<gallery perrow="4" widths="175px" heights="175px"> File:新原・奴山古墳群20号墳.JPG|Tomb No. 20 File:新原・奴山古墳群21号墳.JPG|Tomb No. 21 File:新原・奴山古墳群22号墳.JPG|Tomb No. 22 File:新原・奴山古墳群34～36号墳.JPG|Tombs Nos. 34–36 </gallery>
During the selection process for designation, other tumuli—such as the [[:en:Sakurakyō Kofun|Sakurakyo Kofun]], {{ill|Tōgō Takatsuka Kofun|ja|東郷高塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōgō Takatsuka Kofun|qq=|1=Tōgō Takatsuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24867823}}, and those within the broader [[:en:Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|Tsuya-zaki Kofun Group]] (which encompasses the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group), including the Katsura Mine-no-Hata Kofun and {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyajidake Kofun|qq=}}—were omitted from selection. The Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group was retained because its location, facing the sea ([[:en:Sea|Sea]]) of the [[:en:Genkai Sea|Genkai-nada]], preserves a “landscape integrated with the sea” ([[:en:Cultural landscape|Cultural Landscape]]) that embodies the maritime worship of the [[:en:Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]] (and its associated {{ill|Munakata Sanmeigen|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanmeigen|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}), as explicitly stated in the recommendation documents. For these reasons, the more inland [[:en:Sakurakyō Kofun|Sakurakyo Kofun]] and {{ill|Tōgō Takatsuka Kofun|ja|東郷高塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōgō Takatsuka Kofun|qq=|1=Tōgō Takatsuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24867823}} were excluded, and {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyajidake Kofun|qq=}}—serving as the inner shrine of [[:en:Miyajidake Shrine|Miyajidake Shrine]] and representing a different aspect of Munakata faith—was also left out.
In the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group, a designated viewscape preservation zone under the {{ill|Landscape Act|ja|景観法|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Landscape Act|qq=|1=Landscape Act|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11514692}} has been applied to conserve the agricultural lands, rural scenery, and overall landscape surrounding the tumuli—which overlooks {{ill|Oshima (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|大島 (福岡県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshima (Fukuoka Prefecture)|qq=|1=Ōshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434813}}—and under the {{ill|Outdoor Advertising Law|ja|屋外広告物法|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outdoor Advertising Law|qq=|1=Outdoor Advertisement Act|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465670}} and local ordinances, incongruous buildings and signboards are periodically removed.<ref>[http://www.city.fukutsu.lg.jp/pdf/kenkou/toshi/keikankeikaku01.pdf]{{ill|Fukutsu City Landscape Plan|ja|福津市景観計画|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukutsu City Landscape Plan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref>
== Post-World Heritage Registration Developments == Following the heavy rains in [[:en:2018|Heisei 30]] July, part of the tumuli collapsed.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20180711185327/https://this.kiji.is/389598562214937697?c=39546741839462401 Damage reported in the World Heritage kofun group, Fukuoka, {{ill|Kyodo News|ja|共同通信|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyodo News|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 共同通信社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 共同通信社|1=Kyodo News|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q779730}}, July 11, 2018]</ref>
== Access == * From the Miyaji exit of [[:en:Fukuma Station|Fukuma Station]] on the [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]], take the {{ill|Fukutsu Mini Bus, Katsura Line|ja|ふくつミニバス#勝浦線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukutsu Mini Bus, Katsura Line|qq=|1=Fukutsu Mini Bus, Katsura Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11276990}} and alight at the Showa Gakuen-mae (Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group) stop. However, only three services operate per day from the station, and only three departures run from the Fukutsu City Complex Cultural Center (Camellia Stage)—the guidance facility for the kofun group—toward Katsura-hama (access to Camellia Stage is made via a connection from Fukuma Station on the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|qq=|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} heading to Tsuyazaki). Services do not operate on Sundays, public holidays, or during the New Year period. Because a one-box (minibus) vehicle is used, capacity is limited to 13 people, with priority given to elderly local residents who have voluntarily surrendered their driver's licenses and to those living in areas with insufficient public transport along the route. The fare is a flat rate of 200 yen. Note that the Showa Gakuen-mae (Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group) stop is also the nearest to the Nuyama Observatory, from which one can enjoy a distant view of the kofun group. * By car, it is approximately 13 km from the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]] {{ill|Koga Interchange|ja|古賀インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koga Interchange|qq=|1=Koga interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1577981}}.

[[sn:E6%96%B0%E5%8E%9F%E3%83%BB%E5%A5%B4%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[sn:hinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]]
[[ja:新原・奴山古墳群]] [[sn:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Sacred Island of Okinoshima and Associated Sites in the Munakata Region]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Cemeteries on the World Heritage List]]
[[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Fukutsu]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group}}

{{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Shinbaru-Nuyama Necropolis 20161014.jpg|location={{ill|Fukutsu|qid=Q825700}}|coordinates={{Coord|33.8175|N|130.48583333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|builder={{ill|Munakata clan|qid=Q16481907}}|part_of={{ill|Sacred Island of Okinoshima and Associated Sites in the Munakata Region|qid=Q324638}}; {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Group|qid=Q109595331}}|Historic Site={{ill|part of UNESCO World Heritage Site|qid=Q43113623}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q22119078]] Wikidata name: Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:27, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{More footnotes needed|date=2015th year 8月12日 (水) 16:00 (UTC)}} [[File:Shinbaru-Nuyama Necropolis 20161014.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Stone Monument|ja|木碑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Monument|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group (新原・奴山古墳群)]] [[File:Shimbata-nuyama_kofun.jpg|alt=Distant View [ja] of the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group (新原・奴山古墳群)|thumb]]
'''Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group''' (新原・奴山古墳群) is an assemblage of ancient Kofuns ([[:en:Kofun|Kofun]]) that forms one part of the [[:en:Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|Tsuya-zaki Kofun Group]], located in the Tsushima–Miyan mountain system of [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]’s [[:en:Fukutsu, Fukuoka|Fukutsu City]]. It has been designated a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} (it is included within the Historic Site “[[:en:Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|Tsuya-zaki Kofun Group]]”). The tumuli were constructed from the latter half of the 5th century to the latter half of the 6th century during the middle-late phase of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun Period]], and those interred are presumed to have been members or affiliates of the {{ill|Munakata Clan|ja|宗像氏|1=Munakata clan|fr|Clan Munakata|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|qq=}}. The name of the kofun group derives from the fact that it spans the “Shinbara” area (the local name in Katsura) and the “Nuyama” district.
In [[:en:2017|2017]] ([[:en:2017|Heisei 29]]), as one of the constituent assets of the [[:en:Sacred Island of Okinoshima and Associated Sites in the Munakata Region|Sacred Island of Okinoshima and Associated Sites in the Munakata Region]], the kofun group was inscribed as a {{ill|World Cultural Heritage|ja|世界文化遺産|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/World Cultural Heritage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 文化遺産 (世界遺産)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 文化遺産 (世界遺産)|1=cultural heritage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q210272}}. This marks the first instance in Japan in which a group of kofun has been registered as a World Heritage Site.
== Overview == [http://www.okinoshima-heritage.jp/files/Pamphlet_14_file.pdf] occupies a hilly area stretching 800 meters east–west, on which 5 [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], 1 {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and 41 {{ill|Round Tumuli|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Tumuli|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} currently survive; these have been assigned sequential numbers for convenience. In addition, historical records, excavation surveys ([[:en:Archaeological excavation|Excavation Survey]]), topographical studies ([[:en:Landform|Topography]]) and surveying ([[:en:Surveying|Surveying]]) have confirmed that another 18 tumuli—lost due to the reclamation of adjacent {{ill|paddy fields|ja|田圃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/paddy fields|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田|1=paddy field|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q842623}}—once existed, and these too were assigned sequential numbers.
At the time the tumuli were constructed, an inlet ([[:en:Inlet|Cove]]) adjoined the western side of the hill; this inlet, into which the present {{ill|Tsuyazaki Bay|ja|津屋崎湾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuyazaki Bay|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} penetrated deeply, gradually receded. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo Period]], the area was reclaimed ([[:en:Polder|Land Reclamation]]) and converted into a [[:en:Salt evaporation pond|Salt Field]], and from the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] onward it has been transformed into {{ill|Paddy Fields|ja|水田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Paddy Fields|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田|1=paddy field|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q842623}}.
Around the kofun group, several {{ill|farm ponds|ja|ため池|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farm ponds|qq=|1=irrigation reservoir|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q112083751}}—such as {{ill|Gekka-ike|ja|月花池|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekka-ike|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Harada-ike|ja|原田池|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harada-ike|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Manekike|ja|招池|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manekike|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Fushibara-ike|ja|伏原池|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushibara-ike|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—are scattered; these were constructed for irrigation purposes during the [[:en:Early modern period|Early Modern Period]] and are unrelated to the kofun. The hilly area is bisected by [[:en:Japan National Route 495|National Route 495]]. The basalt ([[:en:Basalt|Basalt]]) used in the tumuli’s stone chambers is presumed to have been transported by boat from [[:en:Ainoshima (Shingū)|Aishima (Fukuoka Prefecture)]] in the [[:en:Genkai Sea|Genkai-nada]].
== List of Tumuli == * '''Tomb No. 1''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with an estimated total length of 50 meters; the square portion is lost. It was constructed in the first half of the 5th century and is covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]]. * '''Tombs No. 2–6''' – These have not survived due to leveling for road construction and private facility development. * '''Tomb No. 7''' – A square tumulus with sides measuring 20–24 meters; it is the only square tumulus in the {{ill|Munakata Region|ja|宗像地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Region|qq=|1=Munakata region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451849}}. Constructed in the first half of the 5th century, it yielded an iron ([[:en:Iron|Iron]]) and an axe ([[:en:Axe|Axe]]) similar to those found in the ritual remains of [[:en:Okinoshima (Fukuoka)|Okinoshima]], suggesting that it may also have served as a ceremonial site. * '''Tombs No. 8–11''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 12''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 43 meters, constructed in the latter half of the 5th century. * '''Tombs No. 13–19''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 20''' – A medium-sized round tumulus covered with fukiishi, constructed in the 5th century. * '''Tomb No. 21''' – A round tumulus with a diameter of 17 meters, constructed in the first half of the 5th century. A stone monument ({{ill|Itaben|ja|板碑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itaben|qq=|1=itabi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11532699}}) from the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura Period]], designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property, stands atop its mound. * '''Tomb No. 22''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with an estimated total length of 80 meters, although the square portion is lost. Constructed in the first half of the 5th century, it is the largest in the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group and Ranks among the oldest. It was also surrounded by moats and embankments, and a stone shrine of the later-enshrined Nuno-den-gu was erected on its mound. * '''Tomb No. 23''' – A small round tumulus from the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 24''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 53 meters, constructed in the first half of the 5th century, and, along with Tomb No. 22, among the oldest. * '''Tomb No. 25''' – A medium-sized round tumulus covered with fukiishi, constructed in the 5th century. * '''Tombs No. 26–29''' – Small round tumuli built on the slopes of the hill, constructed in the latter half of the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 30''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 54 meters, constructed from the mid to late 5th century. * '''Tombs No. 31–43''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tombs Nos. 44–45''' – These have been lost due to leveling. * '''Tombs Nos. 46–48''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tombs Nos. 49–59''' – These have been lost due to the reclamation of paddy fields.
<gallery perrow="4" widths="175px" heights="175px"> File:新原・奴山古墳群20号墳.JPG|Tomb No. 20 File:新原・奴山古墳群21号墳.JPG|Tomb No. 21 File:新原・奴山古墳群22号墳.JPG|Tomb No. 22 File:新原・奴山古墳群34～36号墳.JPG|Tombs Nos. 34–36 </gallery>
During the selection process for designation, other tumuli—such as the [[:en:Sakurakyō Kofun|Sakurakyo Kofun]], {{ill|Tōgō Takatsuka Kofun|ja|東郷高塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōgō Takatsuka Kofun|qq=|1=Tōgō Takatsuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24867823}}, and those within the broader [[:en:Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|Tsuya-zaki Kofun Group]] (which encompasses the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group), including the Katsura Mine-no-Hata Kofun and {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyajidake Kofun|qq=}}—were omitted from selection. The Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group was retained because its location, facing the sea ([[:en:Sea|Sea]]) of the [[:en:Genkai Sea|Genkai-nada]], preserves a “landscape integrated with the sea” ([[:en:Cultural landscape|Cultural Landscape]]) that embodies the maritime worship of the [[:en:Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]] (and its associated {{ill|Munakata Sanmeigen|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanmeigen|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}), as explicitly stated in the recommendation documents. For these reasons, the more inland [[:en:Sakurakyō Kofun|Sakurakyo Kofun]] and {{ill|Tōgō Takatsuka Kofun|ja|東郷高塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōgō Takatsuka Kofun|qq=|1=Tōgō Takatsuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24867823}} were excluded, and {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyajidake Kofun|qq=}}—serving as the inner shrine of [[:en:Miyajidake Shrine|Miyajidake Shrine]] and representing a different aspect of Munakata faith—was also left out.
In the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group, a designated viewscape preservation zone under the {{ill|Landscape Act|ja|景観法|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Landscape Act|qq=|1=Landscape Act|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11514692}} has been applied to conserve the agricultural lands, rural scenery, and overall landscape surrounding the tumuli—which overlooks {{ill|Oshima (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|大島 (福岡県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshima (Fukuoka Prefecture)|qq=|1=Ōshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434813}}—and under the {{ill|Outdoor Advertising Law|ja|屋外広告物法|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outdoor Advertising Law|qq=|1=Outdoor Advertisement Act|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465670}} and local ordinances, incongruous buildings and signboards are periodically removed.<ref>[http://www.city.fukutsu.lg.jp/pdf/kenkou/toshi/keikankeikaku01.pdf]{{ill|Fukutsu City Landscape Plan|ja|福津市景観計画|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukutsu City Landscape Plan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref>
== Post-World Heritage Registration Developments == Following the heavy rains in [[:en:2018|Heisei 30]] July, part of the tumuli collapsed.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20180711185327/https://this.kiji.is/389598562214937697?c=39546741839462401 Damage reported in the World Heritage kofun group, Fukuoka, {{ill|Kyodo News|ja|共同通信|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyodo News|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 共同通信社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 共同通信社|1=Kyodo News|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q779730}}, July 11, 2018]</ref>
== Access == * From the Miyaji exit of [[:en:Fukuma Station|Fukuma Station]] on the [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]], take the {{ill|Fukutsu Mini Bus, Katsura Line|ja|ふくつミニバス#勝浦線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukutsu Mini Bus, Katsura Line|qq=|1=Fukutsu Mini Bus, Katsura Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11276990}} and alight at the Showa Gakuen-mae (Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group) stop. However, only three services operate per day from the station, and only three departures run from the Fukutsu City Complex Cultural Center (Camellia Stage)—the guidance facility for the kofun group—toward Katsura-hama (access to Camellia Stage is made via a connection from Fukuma Station on the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|qq=|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} heading to Tsuyazaki). Services do not operate on Sundays, public holidays, or during the New Year period. Because a one-box (minibus) vehicle is used, capacity is limited to 13 people, with priority given to elderly local residents who have voluntarily surrendered their driver's licenses and to those living in areas with insufficient public transport along the route. The fare is a flat rate of 200 yen. Note that the Showa Gakuen-mae (Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group) stop is also the nearest to the Nuyama Observatory, from which one can enjoy a distant view of the kofun group. * By car, it is approximately 13 km from the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]] {{ill|Koga Interchange|ja|古賀インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koga Interchange|qq=|1=Koga interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1577981}}.

{{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Shinbaru-Nuyama Necropolis 20161014.jpg|location={{ill|Fukutsu|qid=Q825700}}|coordinates={{Coord|33.8175|N|130.48583333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|builder={{ill|Munakata clan|qid=Q16481907}}|part_of={{ill|Sacred Island of Okinoshima and Associated Sites in the Munakata Region|qid=Q324638}}; {{ill|Tsuyazaki Kofun Group|qid=Q109595331}}|Historic Site={{ill|part of UNESCO World Heritage Site|qid=Q43113623}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q22119078]] Wikidata name: Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:27, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{More footnotes needed|date=2015th year 8月12日 (水) 16:00 (UTC)}} [[File:Shinbaru-Nuyama Necropolis 20161014.jpg|thumb|300px|{{ill|Stone Monument|ja|木碑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Monument|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group (新原・奴山古墳群)]] [[File:Shimbata-nuyama_kofun.jpg|alt=Distant View [ja] of the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group (新原・奴山古墳群)|thumb]]
'''Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group''' (新原・奴山古墳群) is an assemblage of ancient Kofuns ([[:en:Kofun|Kofun]]) that forms one part of the [[:en:Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|Tsuya-zaki Kofun Group]], located in the Tsushima–Miyan mountain system of [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]’s [[:en:Fukutsu, Fukuoka|Fukutsu City]]. It has been designated a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} (it is included within the Historic Site “[[:en:Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|Tsuya-zaki Kofun Group]]”). The tumuli were constructed from the latter half of the 5th century to the latter half of the 6th century during the middle-late phase of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun Period]], and those interred are presumed to have been members or affiliates of the {{ill|Munakata Clan|ja|宗像氏|1=Munakata clan|fr|Clan Munakata|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|qq=}}. The name of the kofun group derives from the fact that it spans the “Shinbara” area (the local name in Katsura) and the “Nuyama” district.
In [[:en:2017|2017]] ([[:en:2017|Heisei 29]]), as one of the constituent assets of the [[:en:Sacred Island of Okinoshima and Associated Sites in the Munakata Region|Sacred Island of Okinoshima and Associated Sites in the Munakata Region]], the kofun group was inscribed as a {{ill|World Cultural Heritage|ja|世界文化遺産|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/World Cultural Heritage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 文化遺産 (世界遺産)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 文化遺産 (世界遺産)|1=cultural heritage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q210272}}. This marks the first instance in Japan in which a group of kofun has been registered as a World Heritage Site.
== Overview == [http://www.okinoshima-heritage.jp/files/Pamphlet_14_file.pdf] occupies a hilly area stretching 800 meters east–west, on which 5 [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], 1 {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and 41 {{ill|Round Tumuli|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Tumuli|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} currently survive; these have been assigned sequential numbers for convenience. In addition, historical records, excavation surveys ([[:en:Archaeological excavation|Excavation Survey]]), topographical studies ([[:en:Landform|Topography]]) and surveying ([[:en:Surveying|Surveying]]) have confirmed that another 18 tumuli—lost due to the reclamation of adjacent {{ill|paddy fields|ja|田圃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/paddy fields|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田|1=paddy field|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q842623}}—once existed, and these too were assigned sequential numbers.
At the time the tumuli were constructed, an inlet ([[:en:Inlet|Cove]]) adjoined the western side of the hill; this inlet, into which the present {{ill|Tsuyazaki Bay|ja|津屋崎湾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuyazaki Bay|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} penetrated deeply, gradually receded. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo Period]], the area was reclaimed ([[:en:Polder|Land Reclamation]]) and converted into a [[:en:Salt evaporation pond|Salt Field]], and from the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] onward it has been transformed into {{ill|Paddy Fields|ja|水田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Paddy Fields|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田|1=paddy field|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q842623}}.
Around the kofun group, several {{ill|farm ponds|ja|ため池|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farm ponds|qq=|1=irrigation reservoir|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q112083751}}—such as {{ill|Gekka-ike|ja|月花池|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gekka-ike|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Harada-ike|ja|原田池|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harada-ike|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Manekike|ja|招池|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manekike|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Fushibara-ike|ja|伏原池|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushibara-ike|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—are scattered; these were constructed for irrigation purposes during the [[:en:Early modern period|Early Modern Period]] and are unrelated to the kofun. The hilly area is bisected by [[:en:Japan National Route 495|National Route 495]]. The basalt ([[:en:Basalt|Basalt]]) used in the tumuli’s stone chambers is presumed to have been transported by boat from [[:en:Ainoshima (Shingū)|Aishima (Fukuoka Prefecture)]] in the [[:en:Genkai Sea|Genkai-nada]].
== List of Tumuli == * '''Tomb No. 1''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with an estimated total length of 50 meters; the square portion is lost. It was constructed in the first half of the 5th century and is covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]]. * '''Tombs No. 2–6''' – These have not survived due to leveling for road construction and private facility development. * '''Tomb No. 7''' – A square tumulus with sides measuring 20–24 meters; it is the only square tumulus in the {{ill|Munakata Region|ja|宗像地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Region|qq=|1=Munakata region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451849}}. Constructed in the first half of the 5th century, it yielded an iron ([[:en:Iron|Iron]]) and an axe ([[:en:Axe|Axe]]) similar to those found in the ritual remains of [[:en:Okinoshima (Fukuoka)|Okinoshima]], suggesting that it may also have served as a ceremonial site. * '''Tombs No. 8–11''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 12''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 43 meters, constructed in the latter half of the 5th century. * '''Tombs No. 13–19''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 20''' – A medium-sized round tumulus covered with fukiishi, constructed in the 5th century. * '''Tomb No. 21''' – A round tumulus with a diameter of 17 meters, constructed in the first half of the 5th century. A stone monument ({{ill|Itaben|ja|板碑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itaben|qq=|1=itabi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11532699}}) from the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura Period]], designated as a Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property, stands atop its mound. * '''Tomb No. 22''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with an estimated total length of 80 meters, although the square portion is lost. Constructed in the first half of the 5th century, it is the largest in the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group and Ranks among the oldest. It was also surrounded by moats and embankments, and a stone shrine of the later-enshrined Nuno-den-gu was erected on its mound. * '''Tomb No. 23''' – A small round tumulus from the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 24''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 53 meters, constructed in the first half of the 5th century, and, along with Tomb No. 22, among the oldest. * '''Tomb No. 25''' – A medium-sized round tumulus covered with fukiishi, constructed in the 5th century. * '''Tombs No. 26–29''' – Small round tumuli built on the slopes of the hill, constructed in the latter half of the 6th century. * '''Tomb No. 30''' – A keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 54 meters, constructed from the mid to late 5th century. * '''Tombs No. 31–43''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tombs Nos. 44–45''' – These have been lost due to leveling. * '''Tombs Nos. 46–48''' – Small round tumuli from the 6th century. * '''Tombs Nos. 49–59''' – These have been lost due to the reclamation of paddy fields.
<gallery perrow="4" widths="175px" heights="175px"> File:新原・奴山古墳群20号墳.JPG|Tomb No. 20 File:新原・奴山古墳群21号墳.JPG|Tomb No. 21 File:新原・奴山古墳群22号墳.JPG|Tomb No. 22 File:新原・奴山古墳群34～36号墳.JPG|Tombs Nos. 34–36 </gallery>
During the selection process for designation, other tumuli—such as the [[:en:Sakurakyō Kofun|Sakurakyo Kofun]], {{ill|Tōgō Takatsuka Kofun|ja|東郷高塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōgō Takatsuka Kofun|qq=|1=Tōgō Takatsuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24867823}}, and those within the broader [[:en:Tsuyazaki Kofun Cluster|Tsuya-zaki Kofun Group]] (which encompasses the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group), including the Katsura Mine-no-Hata Kofun and {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyajidake Kofun|qq=}}—were omitted from selection. The Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group was retained because its location, facing the sea ([[:en:Sea|Sea]]) of the [[:en:Genkai Sea|Genkai-nada]], preserves a “landscape integrated with the sea” ([[:en:Cultural landscape|Cultural Landscape]]) that embodies the maritime worship of the [[:en:Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]] (and its associated {{ill|Munakata Sanmeigen|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanmeigen|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}), as explicitly stated in the recommendation documents. For these reasons, the more inland [[:en:Sakurakyō Kofun|Sakurakyo Kofun]] and {{ill|Tōgō Takatsuka Kofun|ja|東郷高塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōgō Takatsuka Kofun|qq=|1=Tōgō Takatsuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24867823}} were excluded, and {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyajidake Kofun|qq=}}—serving as the inner shrine of [[:en:Miyajidake Shrine|Miyajidake Shrine]] and representing a different aspect of Munakata faith—was also left out.
In the Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group, a designated viewscape preservation zone under the {{ill|Landscape Act|ja|景観法|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Landscape Act|qq=|1=Landscape Act|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11514692}} has been applied to conserve the agricultural lands, rural scenery, and overall landscape surrounding the tumuli—which overlooks {{ill|Oshima (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|大島 (福岡県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshima (Fukuoka Prefecture)|qq=|1=Ōshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434813}}—and under the {{ill|Outdoor Advertising Law|ja|屋外広告物法|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outdoor Advertising Law|qq=|1=Outdoor Advertisement Act|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465670}} and local ordinances, incongruous buildings and signboards are periodically removed.<ref>[http://www.city.fukutsu.lg.jp/pdf/kenkou/toshi/keikankeikaku01.pdf]{{ill|Fukutsu City Landscape Plan|ja|福津市景観計画|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukutsu City Landscape Plan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref>
== Post-World Heritage Registration Developments == Following the heavy rains in [[:en:2018|Heisei 30]] July, part of the tumuli collapsed.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20180711185327/https://this.kiji.is/389598562214937697?c=39546741839462401 Damage reported in the World Heritage kofun group, Fukuoka, {{ill|Kyodo News|ja|共同通信|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyodo News|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 共同通信社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 共同通信社|1=Kyodo News|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q779730}}, July 11, 2018]</ref>
== Access == * From the Miyaji exit of [[:en:Fukuma Station|Fukuma Station]] on the [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]], take the {{ill|Fukutsu Mini Bus, Katsura Line|ja|ふくつミニバス#勝浦線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukutsu Mini Bus, Katsura Line|qq=|1=Fukutsu Mini Bus, Katsura Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11276990}} and alight at the Showa Gakuen-mae (Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group) stop. However, only three services operate per day from the station, and only three departures run from the Fukutsu City Complex Cultural Center (Camellia Stage)—the guidance facility for the kofun group—toward Katsura-hama (access to Camellia Stage is made via a connection from Fukuma Station on the {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|qq=|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} heading to Tsuyazaki). Services do not operate on Sundays, public holidays, or during the New Year period. Because a one-box (minibus) vehicle is used, capacity is limited to 13 people, with priority given to elderly local residents who have voluntarily surrendered their driver's licenses and to those living in areas with insufficient public transport along the route. The fare is a flat rate of 200 yen. Note that the Showa Gakuen-mae (Shinbaru-Nuyama Kofun Group) stop is also the nearest to the Nuyama Observatory, from which one can enjoy a distant view of the kofun group. * By car, it is approximately 13 km from the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]] {{ill|Koga Interchange|ja|古賀インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koga Interchange|qq=|1=Koga interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1577981}}.
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Munakata Clan|ja|宗像氏|1=Munakata clan|fr|Clan Munakata|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Miyajidake Kofun|ja|宮地嶽古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyajidake Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Ancient Tombs in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ancient Tombs in Japan|qq=|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|qq=|1=List of historic sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370155}}
== External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/207243] ({{ill|Cultural Heritage Online|ja|文化遺産オンライン|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Heritage Online|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 文化庁|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 文化庁|1=Agency for Cultural Affairs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1009519}}) * [http://www.fsg.pref.fukuoka.jp/bunka/index.asp]
